title: The three-phase skeleton pipeline
text: pre-derived-codes approach tried clever: register ICD-10 codes groups = list(ov, sv, dors) combine_as = "osd", hoping get one column covering three sources. failed silently add_diagnoses searches hdia/dia*/ekod*/icd* columns – none exist cause--death registry. DORS uses ulorsak (underlying cause) morsak* (contributing causes). dors_f20 always FALSE osd_f20 = ov_f20 | sv_f20 | FALSE effectively just hospital data. Deaths never contributed outcomes. fix needs two different functions – add_diagnoses hospital, add_cods DORS – sharing one logical combined column. combine_as can’t express : reruns fn rbind data. need way together columns produced different registrations. Enter register_derived_codes(): derived entry doesn’t read rawbatch data . iterates codes list, looks get("<[1]>_<nm>"), get("<[2]>_<nm>"), etc. skeleton, ORs , writes <>_<nm>. Missing source columns raise loud error. runs registration order phase 2 sync, upstream primary entries guaranteed applied already.
code: # 1. Hospital (OV + SV) -> ov_*, sv_*, os_* study$register_codes(   codes      = ICD10_CODES,   fn         = swereg::add_diagnoses,   groups     = list(ov = "outpatient", sv = "inpatient"),   combine_as = "os" )  # 2. DORS underlying cause -> dorsu_* study$register_codes(   codes   = ICD10_CODES,   fn      = swereg::add_cods,   fn_args = list(cod_type = "underlying"),   groups  = list(dorsu = "dors") )  # 3. DORS contributing causes -> dorsm_* study$register_codes(   codes   = ICD10_CODES,   fn      = swereg::add_cods,   fn_args = list(cod_type = "multiple"),   groups  = list(dorsm = "dors") )  # 4. Derived: osd_<nm> = os_<nm> | dorsu_<nm> | dorsm_<nm> study$register_derived_codes(   codes = ICD10_CODES,   from  = c("os", "dorsu", "dorsm"),   as    = "osd" )

title: The three-phase skeleton pipeline
text: complete runner script shape: critical invariant: framework, randvars, code registry must registered study $process_skeletons() called. callback argument $process_skeletons() – pipeline declared study , passed per call.
code: study <- swereg::RegistryStudy$new(   data_rawbatch_dir = c("/mnt/shared/rawbatch/", "C:/shared/rawbatch/"),   data_skeleton_dir = c("/mnt/shared/skeleton/", "C:/shared/skeleton/"),   data_raw_dir      = c("/mnt/shared/raw/",      "C:/shared/raw/"),   group_names       = c("lmed", "inpatient", "outpatient",                         "cancer", "dors", "other") )  # Phase 1 study$register_framework(my_framework_fn)  # Phase 3 (registration order = execution order) study$register_randvars("demographics",        rv_demographics) study$register_randvars("exposure",            rv_exposure) study$register_randvars("baseline_exclusion",  rv_baseline_exclusion)  # Phase 2: primaries first, then derived study$register_codes(ICD10_CODES, swereg::add_diagnoses,   groups = list(ov = "outpatient", sv = "inpatient"), combine_as = "os") study$register_codes(ICD10_CODES, swereg::add_cods,   fn_args = list(cod_type = "underlying"),   groups = list(dorsu = "dors")) study$register_codes(ICD10_CODES, swereg::add_cods,   fn_args = list(cod_type = "multiple"),   groups = list(dorsm = "dors")) study$register_derived_codes(ICD10_CODES,   from = c("os", "dorsu", "dorsm"), as = "osd")  # ... rawbatch creation (one-time) ...  study$process_skeletons(n_workers = 4L)

title: TTE methodology: mapping to reference papers
text: Hernán & Robins (2016) — “Using big data emulate target trial randomized trial available.” J Epidemiol. Theoretical framework defining four emulation failures solutions. Hernán et al. (2008) — “Observational analyses effect combined estrogen-progestin therapy coronary heart disease.” Epidemiology. original “sequence nested trials” paper. NHS/WHI hormone therapy example. Danaei et al. (2013) — “Statins coronary heart disease events.” Epidemiology. detailed methods paper covering ITT, per-protocol, -treated analyses SAS code. title: TTE methodology: mapping to reference papers
text: Note analysis types: swereg pipeline designed per-protocol analysis. $s4_prepare_for_analysis() applies per-protocol censoring (treatment switching), fundamentally modifies dataset — ITT analysis possible step. separate ITT pipeline need skip per-protocol censoring entirely. -treated analysis requires different weighting framework. Note IPCW stabilization: Danaei (2013) describes stabilized IPCW weights numerator conditioned baseline covariates. implementation uses simpler marginal (population-average) censoring probability numerator. common simplification equivalent baseline covariates limited predictive power censoring.

what: Enrollment band width and residual immortal time bias
title: Target trial emulation nomenclature
text: period_width parameter TTEDesign (default: 4 weeks) controls width enrollment bands. critical methodological parameter trades bias power: Narrower bands (e.g., period_width = 1): Less residual immortal time bias, fewer events per trial larger datasets. Wider bands (e.g., period_width = 4): residual immortal time bias, events per trial better computational efficiency. Caniglia et al. (2023) explicitly discusses trade-: “one-week enrollment period target trial beginning week 36 inappropriate, defining trials day scale may necessary. Generally, defining trials shorter scale reduce residual immortal time bias.” period_width also serves grace period — window treatment initiation assumed occur. studies treatment defined specific time point, period_width = 1 may appropriate. studies treatment initiation gradual, wider window justified (Hernan 2016, Section 4.4).

title: Target trial emulation nomenclature
text: swereg uses per-band stratified matching: within enrollment band, comparator individuals sampled specified ratio (e.g., 1:5) match number intervention individuals. design choice computational efficiency large registry datasets. Alternative approaches described literature: matching, IPW (Danaei 2013, Hernan 2008): Include eligible non-initiators, adjust via propensity score. statistically efficient computationally expensive large registries. Propensity score matching (Danaei 2013, sensitivity analysis): Match estimated propensity score. complex can achieve better balance. approach combines matching (computational tractability) IPW (correct residual confounding within matched set). IPW step re-weights matched sample balance measured confounders.

previous_headings: Target Trial Emulation with swereg > The TTE concept in one page
title: TTE workflow: from skeleton to results
text: Hernán Robins (2016) name four specific failure modes observational analyses suffer randomized trials : Treatment assignment random – people choose intervention differ systematically people don’t. Corrected baseline confounder adjustment, typically via inverse probability treatment weighting (IPW). Time zero well-defined – trial, time zero moment randomization. observational data moment, define one. Swereg uses band-based enrollment: eligible person-week inside enrollment period becomes potential time-zero, creating sequence overlapping trials. Follow-informatively truncated – people drop observation (emigration, death, end data) ways depend baseline time-varying characteristics. Corrected inverse probability censoring weighting (IPCW). Treatment adherent – trial, adherence enforced (non-adherence measured modeled). observational data, people switch treatments time. swereg handles via per-protocol censoring treatment switch, followed IPCW-PP correct selection creates.

previous_headings: Target Trial Emulation with swereg > The swereg TTE model
title: TTE workflow: from skeleton to results
text: TTEPlan holds ETT grid: Cartesian product enrollments × outcomes × follow-durations. One ETT one final analysis-ready dataset. plan knows run two loops produce datasets. Every row plan$ett points three files: file_raw (post-enrollment, pre-imputation), file_imp (post-imputation + IPW, reused across outcomes within enrollment_id), file_analysis (one per ETT). Loop 1 produces first two; Loop 2 produces third.
code: # spec is a YAML file; study is a RegistryStudy with built skeletons plan <- swereg::tteplan_from_spec_and_registrystudy(   spec  = "my_study/spec.yaml",   study = study ) plan$ett #>     ett_id enrollment_id  outcome_var  follow_up_weeks  file_raw  file_imp  file_analysis #>  1:  ETT01            01  osd_i21_to_i24             52  ...       ...       ... #>  2:  ETT02            01  osd_i21_to_i24            156  ...       ...       ... #>  3:  ETT03            01  osd_i21_to_i24            260  ...       ...       ... #>  4:  ETT04            01  osd_i60_i61_i63            52  ...       ...       ... #>  5:  ETT05            01  osd_i60_i61_i63           156  ...       ...       ... #> ...

title: TTE workflow: from skeleton to results
text: item exclusion_criteria two halves: clinical half (name, rationale) medical collaborators can review without knowing anything R. implementation half (implementation:) swereg consumes: name skeleton column used evaluate rule (source_variable), time window event column causes exclusion (window), computed flag marking rules swereg apply automatically enrollment time via rolling window. window can : integer number weeks (e.g. 104 “past 104 weeks”). string "lifetime_before_baseline" (time candidate time zero). string "lifetime_before_and_after_baseline" (ever, past future). distinction “lifetime_before” “lifetime_before_and_after” matters prevalent vs incident outcomes: prior myocardial infarction excludes looking backward (forward outcome), condition fundamentally confounds treatment choice regardless timing exclude looking directions.

title: TTE workflow: from skeleton to results
text: item enrollments one sequence trials. share global inclusion/exclusion spec add additional_inclusion (almost always age range) additional_exclusion (). treatment.implementation block names skeleton column classifies treatment (rd_statin_status example, string column values like "initiated", "not_initiated"), value counts intervention arm, value counts comparator, per-band sampling ratio.

title: TTE workflow: from skeleton to results
text: plan$s1_generate_enrollments_and_ipw() runs one iteration per enrollment_id (per ETT – several ETTs different outcomes can share enrollment). iteration spawns callr subprocess : Loads skeleton files batches assigned enrollment. Applies global inclusion filter (isoyears) global exclusion criteria via tteplan_apply_exclusions(). Applies enrollment’s additional_inclusion / additional_exclusion. Computes computed: true confounder columns via tteplan_apply_derived_confounders(). Creates TTEEnrollment matching calls $s1_collapse() drop empty rows. Multiply-imputes missing confounder values via $s2_impute_confounders(). Fits baseline IPW model (stabilized logistic regression treatment confounders) via $s3_ipw(). Truncates extreme weights 1/99 percentiles via $s4_truncate_weights(). Saves result file_imp ({prefix}_imp_{enrollment_id}.qs2). Step 2 swereg enforces every skeleton consumed plan pipeline-consistent. Near top tteplan_from_spec_and_registrystudy() call study$assert_skeletons_consistent(), batch’s pipeline_hash differs study’s current pipeline hash, plan construction errors loudly. prevents failure mode edit code ICD10_CODES, rebuild skeletons batches, silently run analysis half-upgraded pipeline. Steps 5-7 methods TTEEnrollment: Loop 1 produces two files per enrollment_id: file_raw intermediate file_imp final. reason split imputation stochastic; file_raw intermediate lets audit raw matched trial panel imputation, useful diagnosing mismatches protocol.
code: enrollment <- TTEEnrollment$new(   data   = person_week_data,   design = my_design,   ratio  = 2 ) enrollment$enrollment_stage   # "pre_enrollment"  # Inside TTEEnrollment$new() when `ratio` is passed: # $enroll() is called privately -- this is where person-week # becomes trial. `data_level` transitions "person_week" -> "trial".  enrollment$s1_collapse()      # drops empty rows enrollment$s2_impute_confounders() enrollment$s3_ipw() enrollment$s4_truncate_weights()  enrollment$enrollment_stage   # "enrolled"

title: TTE workflow: from skeleton to results
text: plan$s2_generate_analysis_files_and_ipcw_pp() runs one iteration per row plan$ett (one per outcome × follow-combination). iteration: Loads file_imp row’s enrollment_id. Imputed confounders baseline IPW weights already . Prepares outcome: joins outcome column, censors first event end follow-window. Fits IPCW-PP model (GAM/GLM censoring time-varying covariates) via $s5_prepare_for_analysis(). includes per-protocol censoring treatment switch, output ITT dataset. Combines weights: analysis_weight_pp = ipw * ipcw_pp. Truncates drops intermediate IPCW columns. Saves result file_analysis ({prefix}_analysis_{ett_id}.qs2). Loop 2 runs sequentially main process (parallelized), ETT’s cost usually dominated /O outcome + IPCW-PP models fast. ever stops true parallelization straightforward extension.

what: The analysis file: rates, IRRs, KM curves
title: TTE workflow: from skeleton to results
text: Loop 2, file_analysis trial-level panel truncated combined weights ready effect estimation. TTEEnrollment R6 class provides three estimation methods wrap survey::svyglm survey::svykm right design specification: IRR approximation hazard ratio used alternative, survey::svycoxph(), computationally prohibitive registry scale. rare outcomes (dominate TTE studies) quasi-Poisson IRR splines::ns(tstop, df = 3) close approximation flexible-baseline Cox model (Thompson 1977).
code: enrollment <- swereg::qs2_read(x_file_analysis)  # Weighted rates (per person-year) by arm, trial_id, etc. enrollment$rates(   weight_col = "analysis_weight_pp_trunc",   by = c("treatment") )  # Weighted incidence rate ratio via quasipoisson # (pooled logistic / IRR-as-HR approximation) enrollment$irr(   weight_col = "analysis_weight_pp_trunc",   formula    = outcome ~ treatment + splines::ns(tstop, df = 3) + trial_id )  # Weighted Kaplan-Meier with person-level clustered SEs enrollment$km(   weight_col = "analysis_weight_pp_trunc" )

title: TTE workflow: from skeleton to results
text: typical project script shape: x_ prefix loop-extracted variables swereg convention (x_outcome outcome) loop scalars always visually distinct dataset columns inside body loop.
code: # 1. Load the study built by the skeleton pipeline study <- swereg::registrystudy_load(data_rawbatch_candidates)  # 2. Build the plan from the spec + study plan <- swereg::tteplan_from_spec_and_registrystudy(   spec  = "my_study/spec.yaml",   study = study )  # 3. Loop 1: enrollment + IPW (parallel across enrollment_ids) plan$s1_generate_enrollments_and_ipw(n_workers = 4L)  # 4. Loop 2: per-ETT outcome weighting (sequential, fast) plan$s2_generate_analysis_files_and_ipcw_pp()  # 5. Per-ETT analysis for (i in seq_len(nrow(plan$ett))) {   x_ett_id         <- plan$ett$ett_id[i]   x_file_analysis  <- plan$ett$file_analysis[i]   x_outcome        <- plan$ett$outcome_var[i]    enrollment <- swereg::qs2_read(x_file_analysis)    irr_result <- enrollment$irr(     weight_col = "analysis_weight_pp_trunc",     formula    = as.formula(sprintf("%s ~ treatment + splines::ns(tstop, df = 3) + trial_id", x_outcome))   )   # save irr_result to results/ ... }

Together, give reproducibility chain final estimates back exact code built time grid, applied phase-3 randvars, registered code entries. running Loop 1, plan asserts skeletons consistent (study$assert_skeletons_consistent()). passes, ’re guaranteed every batch built framework + randvars + codes configuration. spec_vNNN.yaml file committed pipeline snapshot TSV committed, git log can answer “different run run B” without guesswork.

title: TTE workflow: from skeleton to results
text: Three R6 classes: TTEDesign (schema), TTEEnrollment (one sequence trials), TTEPlan (ETT grid builder). Two loops: Loop 1 enrollment + baseline IPW per enrollment_id parallel callr workers; Loop 2 per-protocol censoring + IPCW-PP per ETT sequentially. Spec YAML captures every clinical methodological decision file medical collaborators can review swereg pipeline can execute. Provenance – pipeline_hash, assert_skeletons_consistent, per-host pipeline snapshots – makes reproducibility chain auditable. Weights: baseline IPW confounder adjustment (Loop 1), IPCW-PP per-protocol censoring (Loop 2), combined analysis_weight_pp = ipw * ipcw_pp, truncated 1/99 percentiles. Estimation: quasi-Poisson IRR approximation hazard ratio splines::ns(tstop, df = 3) flexible baseline person-level clustered standard errors via survey. reading: vignette("skeleton-pipeline") – skeleton files consumed TTEPlan built incrementally rebuilt. vignette("tte-methodology") – mapping reference papers. vignette("tte-nomenclature") – one-page glossary. ?TTEPlan, ?TTEEnrollment, ?TTEDesign – full method reference.

title: Manipulating Swedish Registry Data
text: swereg builds longitudinal person-week skeletons Swedish healthcare registry data runs target trial emulations top . centerpiece incrementally-rebuildable three-phase skeleton pipeline: editing one ICD-10 code production setup costs minutes instead rebuilding every batch scratch.

title: Manipulating Swedish Registry Data
text: Builds person-week skeletons NPR (inpatient/outpatient), LMED (prescriptions), DORS (cause death), LISA (demographics), cancer registry, quality registries. Skeleton rows one per person per ISO week, derived columns diagnoses, medications, operations, time-varying confounders. Orchestrates declarative three-phase pipeline (framework / randvars / codes) phase fingerprint-based invalidation strategy. Editing one phase-2 code entry drops entry’s columns persisted skeletons re-applies entry. Editing one phase-3 randvars step triggers divergence-point rewind--replay step everything downstream . Editing framework rebuilds scratch. Runs target trial emulations (Hernan & Robins 2016 / Danaei 2013 style) via YAML spec file + R6 TTEPlan builds ETT grid, runs parallel Loop 1 (enrollment + baseline IPW) sequential Loop 2 (per-protocol censoring + IPCW-PP), produces per-ETT analysis files ready weighted Poisson IRR estimation. Survives multi-host deployments storing directory candidates CandidatePath fields resolve lazily per host, registrystudy.qs2 file works across Linux Windows mounts shared data drive without edits.

title: Manipulating Swedish Registry Data
text: just want play core concept without setting full production pipeline, low-level functions work . right entry point learning, prototyping, small ad-hoc analyses. full manual walkthrough see vignette("skeleton-create").
code: library(data.table) data("fake_person_ids", package = "swereg") data("fake_demographics", package = "swereg") data("fake_diagnoses", package = "swereg")  # 1. Build a person-week time grid for 100 people skeleton <- swereg::create_skeleton(   ids      = fake_person_ids[1:100],   date_min = "2020-01-01",   date_max = "2022-12-31" )  # 2. Normalize column names + parse dates swereg::make_lowercase_names(fake_demographics, date_columns = "fodelseman") swereg::make_lowercase_names(fake_diagnoses,    date_columns = "indatum")  # 3. Attach demographics (one row per person) and diagnoses swereg::add_onetime(   skeleton,   fake_demographics[lopnr %in% fake_person_ids[1:100]],   id_name = "lopnr" ) swereg::add_diagnoses(   skeleton,   fake_diagnoses[lopnr %in% fake_person_ids[1:100]],   id_name = "lopnr",   codes = list(     diabetes   = c("E10", "E11"),     depression = c("F32", "F33")   ) )  head(skeleton)

title: Manipulating Swedish Registry Data
text: Real projects use RegistryStudy top-level orchestrator. pipeline declared study via three families registration calls $process_skeletons() runs. first run builds everything; subsequent runs re-runs phases changed. register_derived_codes() call swereg expresses “combined osd_* column together outputs OV/SV add_diagnoses registration flavors add_cods DORS”. can’t done combine_as alone runs fn rbind data – add_diagnoses searches hdia/dia* add_cods searches ulorsak/morsak*, need different registrations. Derived fingerprints cascade upstream primary edits (e.g. flipping cod_type = "underlying" "") osd_* columns re-compute correctly without user touching derived entry. See vignette("skeleton-pipeline") full mechanics, including divergence-point rewind--replay semantics phase 3 per-entry fingerprint diff phase 2.
code: study <- swereg::RegistryStudy$new(   data_rawbatch_dir = c(     "/mnt/shared/registry/2026/rawbatch/",     "C:/shared/registry/2026/rawbatch/"   ),   data_skeleton_dir = c(     "/mnt/shared/registry/2026/skeleton/",     "C:/shared/registry/2026/skeleton/"   ),   group_names = c("lmed", "inpatient", "outpatient", "cancer", "dors") )  # Phase 1 -- framework: build the base time grid + structural censoring study$register_framework(my_framework_fn)  # Phase 3 -- randvars: ordered user-supplied steps # (registration order = execution order) study$register_randvars("demographics", my_demographics_fn) study$register_randvars("exposure",     my_exposure_fn)  # Phase 2 -- codes: declarative primary + derived registrations study$register_codes(   codes      = ICD10_CODES,   fn         = swereg::add_diagnoses,   groups     = list(ov = "outpatient", sv = "inpatient"),   combine_as = "os" ) study$register_codes(   codes   = ICD10_CODES,   fn      = swereg::add_cods,   fn_args = list(cod_type = "underlying"),   groups  = list(dorsu = "dors") ) study$register_codes(   codes   = ICD10_CODES,   fn      = swereg::add_cods,   fn_args = list(cod_type = "multiple"),   groups  = list(dorsm = "dors") ) # Build osd_<nm> := os_<nm> | dorsu_<nm> | dorsm_<nm> # (the combined outcome column the TTE spec consumes) study$register_derived_codes(   codes = ICD10_CODES,   from  = c("os", "dorsu", "dorsm"),   as    = "osd" )  # First run: build everything. Subsequent runs: incremental. study$process_skeletons(n_workers = 4L)  # Per-batch provenance verification sk <- study$load_skeleton(1L) sk$pipeline_hash() == study$pipeline_hash()   # TRUE iff in sync study$assert_skeletons_consistent()           # errors on mixed state

title: Manipulating Swedish Registry Data
text: Given built skeleton pipeline study spec YAML, swereg can run full causal-inference workflow: spec YAML captures every clinical methodological decision file medical collaborators can review: inclusion / exclusion criteria rationales time windows, confounders (including rolling-window computed ones), outcomes, follow-durations, per-enrollment treatment definitions matching ratios. full TTE walkthrough (epi rationale + R6 mechanics + spec anatomy + two loops + estimation) see vignette("tte-workflow").
code: plan <- swereg::tteplan_from_spec_and_registrystudy(   spec  = "my_study/spec.yaml",   study = study )  # Loop 1: per-enrollment_id in parallel callr workers #   enroll + collapse + impute + IPW + truncate -> file_imp plan$s1_generate_enrollments_and_ipw(n_workers = 4L)  # Loop 2: per-ETT sequential in the main process #   outcome prep + per-protocol censoring + IPCW-PP #   + analysis_weight_pp = ipw * ipcw_pp -> file_analysis plan$s2_generate_analysis_files_and_ipcw_pp()  # Per-ETT estimation for (i in seq_len(nrow(plan$ett))) {   enrollment <- swereg::qs2_read(plan$ett$file_analysis[i])   enrollment$irr(weight_col = "analysis_weight_pp_trunc")   enrollment$rates()   enrollment$km() }

title: Manipulating Swedish Registry Data
text: swereg’s vignettes meant read roughly order. grouping matches pkgdown Articles menu package website. Concept – build mental model touching anything: vignette("skeleton-concept") – person-week time grid beats ad-hoc wide-format builds. vignette("r6-class-overview") – top-tour six R6 classes (CandidatePath, RegistryStudy, Skeleton, TTEDesign, TTEEnrollment, TTEPlan) data-flow diagram showing owns step. vignette("rowdep-rowind-concept") – rd_ (row-dependent) vs ri_ (row-independent) variable prefix conventions rd_ -> ri_ transformation patterns. Pipeline – two production workflows: vignette("skeleton-pipeline") – three-phase skeleton pipeline (framework / randvars / codes), Skeleton R6 class, incremental invalidation, derived codes, pipeline_hash provenance, reload anti-pattern fix. vignette("tte-workflow") – target trial emulation spec YAML per-ETT estimates, epi rationale R6 mechanics page. TTE reference – dive-deep material TTE users reading workflow: vignette("tte-nomenclature") – one-page glossary TTE terms used throughout swereg. vignette("tte-methodology") – mapping reference papers (Hernan 2008/2016, Danaei 2013, Caniglia 2023, TARGET 2025). Manual workflow – low-level entry point small ad-hoc analyses don’t need three-phase pipeline: vignette("skeleton-create") – build skeleton hand create_skeleton() + add_diagnoses() + derived variables. vignette("skeleton-analyze") – collapse person-years run analyses finished skeleton. Cookbooks – worked end--end examples: vignette("cookbook-survival-analysis") – survival analysis walkthrough.

title: Manipulating Swedish Registry Data
text: RegistryStudy$new(...) – constructor taking candidate directory lists + batch config. $register_framework(fn) – phase 1: base time grid + censoring. $register_randvars(name, fn) – phase 3: ordered derived variables (demographics, exposure classification, exclusions). $register_codes(codes, fn, groups, fn_args, combine_as) – phase 2: primary code registrations (add_diagnoses, add_cods, add_operations, add_rx, add_icdo3s, add_quality_registry). $register_derived_codes(codes, , ) – phase 2: derived registrations together upstream primary columns. $process_skeletons(batches, n_workers) – run three phases incremental invalidation. $load_skeleton() / $save_skeleton(sk) / $pipeline_hash() / $assert_skeletons_consistent() – skeleton /O + provenance. $adopt_runtime_state_from() – safe reload pattern (never overwrite study <- qs2_read(...) naive way).

title: Manipulating Swedish Registry Data
text: create_skeleton() – build person-week time grid. make_lowercase_names() – normalize column names + parse Swedish dates (4/6/8-character precision). add_onetime() – baseline/demographic data (one row per person). add_annual() – annual data specific years. add_diagnoses() – NPR diagnosis code matching. add_operations() – NPR surgical procedure codes. add_rx() – LMED prescription data ATC / product codes. add_cods() – DORS cause death (cod_type = "underlying" / "multiple" / ""). add_icdo3s() – cancer registry topography codes. add_quality_registry() – Riksstroke similar quality registries structured-field filters.

title: Manipulating Swedish Registry Data
text: tteplan_from_spec_and_registrystudy(spec, study) – build TTEPlan spec YAML built RegistryStudy. tteplan_read_spec(path) – parse + validate spec YAML. TTEPlan$s1_generate_enrollments_and_ipw() – Loop 1 (enroll + IPW). TTEPlan$s2_generate_analysis_files_and_ipcw_pp() – Loop 2 (outcome + IPCW-PP). TTEEnrollment$rates() / $irr() / $km() / $heterogeneity_test() – per-ETT estimation. TTEPlan$print_target_checklist() – pre-populated TARGET 2025 reporting checklist.

title: Manipulating Swedish Registry Data
text: Swedish registry projects typically run several hosts (Linux workstations, Windows laptops, shared compute servers) data mounted different paths . swereg handles via CandidatePath: every directory field RegistryStudy (TTEPlan) priority list candidate paths, first existing one current host wins. study object serialized via $save_meta(), CandidatePath caches cleared, resulting registrystudy.qs2 loads correctly host.

title: CandidatePath: a directory with multiple candidate locations — CandidatePath
text: Holds priority-ordered list candidate paths directory may live different filesystem locations different hosts (e.g. shared drive mounted different points Linux vs Windows). `$resolve()` returns first candidate exists, caching result subsequent calls free. candidate exists candidate's parent directory , `$resolve()` creates candidate returns – "first run fresh host" gets directory automatically. `CandidatePath` single type used swereg R6 classes ([RegistryStudy], [TTEPlan]) multi-host directory knowledge. classes hold `CandidatePath` instances public fields, resolution behavior structurally identical – drift. cache host-specific must persist across save/load cycle. Containing classes call [invalidate_candidate_paths()] `$save()` methods serialization clear .

title: CandidatePath: a directory with multiple candidate locations — CandidatePath
text: `$new(candidates, label = NULL)` Construct character vector     candidate paths. `$resolve()` Return cached resolved path; otherwise walk     candidate list pick first exists (create first     whose parent exists), cache, return. `$invalidate()` Clear cache. next `$resolve()` call     re-walks candidate list. `$is_resolved()` `TRUE` cache populated cached     path still exists; `FALSE` otherwise. `$print()` Show candidate list, marking cached-resolved     entry `>`.

what: RegistryStudy: Unified R6 class for skeleton pipeline — RegistryStudy
title: RegistryStudy: Unified R6 class for skeleton pipeline — RegistryStudy
text: Manages full skeleton pipeline lifecycle: portable batch directories, batch splitting, raw registry loading, declarative code registry, three-phase orchestrated per-batch processing (framework -> randvars -> codes) produces one [Skeleton] file per batch incremental invalidation.

title: RegistryStudy: Unified R6 class for skeleton pipeline — RegistryStudy
text: Directories stored candidate path vectors resolved lazily via [CandidatePath] active bindings. first existing directory wins cached. cached path becomes invalid (e.g. moving different machine), binding automatically re-resolves candidate list.

title: RegistryStudy: Unified R6 class for skeleton pipeline — RegistryStudy
text: `$process_skeletons()` runs three phases per batch, per-phase incremental invalidation editing one step re-runs affects: Phase 1 – framework single user function registered via     `$register_framework(fn)`, signature `(batch_data, config)`, returns     fresh base `data.table` (time grid + structural censoring).     Full rebuild `body(fn)` / `formals(fn)` hash change. Phase 3 – randvars ordered named list user functions     registered via `$register_randvars(name, fn)`, signature     `(skeleton, batch_data, config)`. Divergence-point rewind--replay     invalidation: first step whose name hash differs     stored sequence triggers drop columns replay plus     everything downstream . Add/remove/edit/reorder handled     uniformly. Phase 2 – codes declarative code registry, built via     `$register_codes()` (primary) `$register_derived_codes()`     (derived). Per-entry fingerprint diff: entries longer present     dropped, new modified entries freshly applied. Derived     entry fingerprints fold upstream primary fingerprints     upstream behavior edits cascade correctly. Phase 2 runs phase 3, phase-3 steps read phase-2 columns. See [Skeleton] class -disk provenance format.

title: RegistryStudy: Unified R6 class for skeleton pipeline — RegistryStudy
text: Primary entries registered via `$register_codes()`, declares codes, function apply (e.g. `add_diagnoses`, `add_cods`), rawbatch groups use, optional prefixing/combining. Derived entries registered via `$register_derived_codes()` together already-existing skeleton columns upstream primary entries – useful combined column needs draw registrations use DIFFERENT `fn`s (something `combine_as` express re-runs `fn` rbound data).

title: RegistryStudy: Unified R6 class for skeleton pipeline — RegistryStudy
text: group_names Character vector. Names rawbatch groups. batch_size Integer. Number IDs per batch. seed Integer. Shuffle seed reproducibility. id_col Character. Person ID column name. n_ids Integer. Total number IDs across batches. n_batches Integer. Number batches. batch_id_list List ID vectors, one per batch. groups_saved Character vector rawbatch groups saved disk. code_registry List code registration entries, appended `$register_codes()` `$register_derived_codes()`. Primary entries (`$register_codes()`) plain lists : `codes, fn, fn_args, groups, combine_as, label`. Derived entries (`$register_derived_codes()`) tagged `kind = "derived"` hold `codes, , , label` instead – `fn`, `groups`, raw data access. dispatcher `.apply_code_entry_impl()` branches entry's `kind` field, defaulting `"primary"` absent. created_at POSIXct. Timestamp study created. data_rawbatch_cp [CandidatePath] rawbatch directory. data_skeleton_cp [CandidatePath] skeleton directory. data_meta_cp [CandidatePath] metadata directory (holds `registrystudy.qs2`). Defaults rawbatch directory backward compatibility. data_raw_cp [CandidatePath] raw-registry directory, NULL configured. data_pipeline_snapshot_cp [CandidatePath] pipeline-snapshot directory (one TSV file per host, git-tracked), NULL feature configured. NULL, `$write_pipeline_snapshot()` silent -op. data_summaries_cp [CandidatePath] audit-track summaries directory (git-tracked TSV per full run), NULL feature configured. NULL, `$compute_summary()` still writes local `summary.qs2` `status.txt` skips TSV. framework_fn Function signature `(batch_data, config)` returning fresh base skeleton `data.table` (phase 1). Set via `$register_framework()`. `$process_skeletons()` re-runs function per batch body/formals hash changes. randvars_fns Named ordered list phase-3 functions, signature `(skeleton, batch_data, config)`. Populated via `$register_randvars(name, fn)`. Registration order execution order. `$process_skeletons()` uses `Skeleton$sync_randvars()`'s divergence-point rewind--replay apply changes incrementally. host_label Optional character scalar. Overrides `Sys.info()[["nodename"]]` naming per-host pipeline snapshot file. Useful hostnames ambiguous overly dynamic. population_by_specs List character vectors. element declares one `` aggregation pre-computed `$process_skeletons()` stored batch's meta sidecar, `$population(= ...)` fast meta-walk. Read back `$population(= <one declared specs>)`.

title: RegistryStudy: Unified R6 class for skeleton pipeline — RegistryStudy
text: data_rawbatch_dir Character (read-). Resolved rawbatch directory current host. Lazily resolved `self$data_rawbatch_cp`. data_skeleton_dir Character (read-). Resolved skeleton directory current host. data_meta_dir Character (read-). Resolved metadata directory current host (`registrystudy.qs2` lives). data_raw_dir Character NULL (read-). Resolved raw-registry directory, NULL configured. data_pipeline_snapshot_dir Character NULL (read-). Resolved pipeline-snapshot directory current host, NULL configured (snapshot feature disabled). data_summaries_dir Character NULL (read-). Resolved audit-track summaries directory current host, NULL configured. skeleton_files Character vector (read-). Skeleton output file paths detected disk. Scans `skeleton_dir` access. expected_skeleton_file_count Integer (read-). Expected number skeleton files (one per batch). meta_file Character. Path -disk metadata file (`registrystudy.qs2`) inside `data_meta_dir`. summary List NULL (read-). `summary.qs2` payload written `$process_skeletons()` (per-column counts, registry-wide totals, build metadata). NULL one-line message file missing.

Example: `list(c("rd_age_continuous"), c("rd_age_continuous", "ri_is_amab"))`. Default: empty list.

Primary entries: fingerprint depends   `(codes, label, groups, fn_args, combine_as)` – two primary   entries identical config produce fingerprint   therefore treated "entry" across runs. Derived entries: fingerprint depends `(codes, , )` PLUS   fingerprints every upstream primary entry whose output   prefix referenced ``. cascades invalidation   upstream primary's `fn_args` / `groups` / `codes` change,   without requiring user touch derived entry. Computed   two-pass walk: primary fingerprints first, derived   fingerprints using already-computed upstream fingerprints. Used `Skeleton$sync_with_registry()` incremental   per-entry add/drop.

title: RegistryStudy: Unified R6 class for skeleton pipeline — RegistryStudy
text: Orchestrate three-phase skeleton pipeline per batch. Reads `self$framework_fn` (phase 1), `self$randvars_fns` (phase 3),   `self$code_registry` (phase 2) study applies   via incremental logic [Skeleton]. Exact per-batch work: 1. Load existing skeleton via `self$load_skeleton()`. missing      `framework_fn_hash` match current      framework's hash, rebuild base skeleton scratch      calling `self$framework_fn(batch_data, self)` wrapping      fresh [Skeleton]. (Phase 1.)   2. Call `sk$sync_randvars()` current ordered      `self$randvars_fns` body/formals hashes. Divergence-      point rewind--replay semantics drop re-run      affected phase-3 steps . (Phase 3.)   3. Call `sk$sync_with_registry()`      `self$code_registry_fingerprints()`. Entries present disk      current registry dropped (via      `.entry_columns()` stored descriptor); entries present      current registry disk applied fresh.      (Phase 2.)   4. Save via `self$save_skeleton(sk)`. `batch_data` loaded lazily – exactly per batch,   whichever phase needs first. phase needs (everything   already sync), rawbatch read skipped entirely   per-batch work just load → save. end full batch loop, `self$write_pipeline_snapshot()`   called (silently -ops `data_pipeline_snapshot_cp`   NULL).

Primary entries first, derived entries after. study$register_codes(   codes      = list(f20 = c("F20"), vte = c("I26", "I80")),   fn         = swereg::add_diagnoses,   groups     = list(ov = "outpatient", sv = "inpatient"),   combine_as = "os" ) study$register_codes(   codes   = list(f20 = c("F20"), vte = c("I26", "I80")),   fn      = swereg::add_cods,   fn_args = list(cod_type = "underlying"),   groups  = list(dorsu = "dors") ) study$register_codes(   codes   = list(f20 = c("F20"), vte = c("I26", "I80")),   fn      = swereg::add_cods,   fn_args = list(cod_type = "multiple"),   groups  = list(dorsm = "dors") ) # Build osd_f20 = os_f20 | dorsu_f20 | dorsm_f20 (same codes list # shared by reference so an edit in one place cascades to all four) study$register_derived_codes(   codes = list(f20 = c("F20"), vte = c("I26", "I80")),   from  = c("os", "dorsu", "dorsm"),   as    = "osd" )  study$set_ids(ids) study$save_rawbatch("lmed", lmed_data) study$describe_codes() study$process_skeletons(n_workers = 4L)  # Per-batch provenance and cross-batch consistency check sk <- study$load_skeleton(1L) sk$pipeline_hash() == study$pipeline_hash()  # TRUE iff in sync study$assert_skeletons_consistent()          # errors on mixed state } # }

text: Bring skeleton sync currently-   registered phase-3 step sequence (phase 3   `$process_skeletons()`). Uses "divergence-point + rewind replay" semantics:   1. Scan stored step sequence (`names(self$randvars_state)` +      stored `fn_hash`s) current sequence      (`names(randvars_fns)` + `randvars_hashes`). Find first      position name hash differs, one sequence      ends.   2. Rewind: drop stored `added_columns` every step      divergence point forward, stored order.   3. Replay: run current steps divergence point      forward, current order, recording step's hash + new      `added_columns`. handles add, remove, edit, reorder uniformly   operations changes either name sequence hash   sequence, first mismatch point divergence point.   divergence exists, method -op   `batch_data_loader` never called.

what: TTEEnrollment class for target trial emulation — TTEEnrollment
title: TTEEnrollment class for target trial emulation — TTEEnrollment
text: Holds enrollment data, design specification, workflow state. Methods modify -place return `invisible(self)` `$`-chaining. R6 reference semantics mean `trial$data[, := ...]` modifies data.table -place without copy--write overhead. `data_level` property controls methods available: - `"person_week"`: Data one row per person per time unit. Pass `ratio`   constructor enroll transition trial level. - `"trial"`: Data expanded trial panels (band-level). Methods   `$s2_ipw()`, `$s4_prepare_for_analysis()`, `$s3_truncate_weights()` require level. Enrollment (matching + panel expansion) transitions data "person_week" "trial" level triggered passing `ratio` constructor.

title: TTEEnrollment class for target trial emulation — TTEEnrollment
text: **Mutating (return `invisible(self)` chaining, step-numbered execution order):** `$s1_impute_confounders(confounder_vars, seed)` Step 1: Impute missing confounders `$s2_ipw(stabilize)` Step 2: Calculate inverse probability treatment weights `$s3_truncate_weights(weight_cols, lower, upper, suffix)` Step 3: Truncate extreme weights `$s4_prepare_for_analysis(outcome, follow_up, ...)` Step 4: Prepare outcome data calculate IPCW-PP one step **Non-mutating (return data):** `$extract()` Return data.table `$summary(pretty)` Return summary statistics `$weight_summary()` Print weight distribution diagnostics `$table1(ipw_col)` Generate baseline characteristics table `$rates(weight_col)` Calculate events, person-years, rates `$irr(weight_col)` Fit Poisson models extract IRR `$km(ipw_col, save_path, title)` Fit Kaplan-Meier curves **Active bindings:** `$enrollment_stage` Derived lifecycle stage: `"pre_enrollment"`, `"enrolled"`, `"analysis_ready"`

title: TTEEnrollment class for target trial emulation — TTEEnrollment
text: data data.table trial data. design TTEDesign R6 object. data_level Character, "person_week" "trial". steps_completed Character vector completed workflow steps. active_outcome Character NULL, current outcome IPCW-PP. weight_cols Character vector weight column names.

title: TTEEnrollment class for target trial emulation — TTEEnrollment
text: enrollment_stage Derived lifecycle stage (read-). Returns `"pre_enrollment"` `data_level == "person_week"`, `"analysis_ready"` `s5_prepare_outcome` run, `"enrolled"` otherwise.

title: TTEEnrollment class for target trial emulation — TTEEnrollment
text: data data.table containing trial data. copy made automatically avoid modifying caller's data. design [TTEDesign] object specifying column mappings. data_level Character NULL. NULL (default), auto-detects based identifier column exists data. "person_week" pre-panel data (requires person_id_var), "trial" post-panel data (requires id_var). steps_completed Character vector completed workflow steps. active_outcome Character NULL, current outcome IPCW-PP analysis. weight_cols Character vector weight column names created. ratio Numeric NULL. provided, automatically enrolls participants (sampling comparison group creating trial panels). valid person_week data. seed Integer NULL. Random seed enrollment reproducibility. extra_cols Character vector NULL. Extra columns include trial panels enrollment. enrolled_ids data.table NULL. Pre-matched enrollment IDs two-pass pipeline. provided, enrollment skips matching phase uses IDs directly. own_data Logical. TRUE, takes ownership data.table without copying . Use caller reuse data.

text: Step 2: Calculates inverse probability treatment weights. Estimates propensity score P(=1 | L_baseline) via logistic regression baseline rows , computes stabilized (unstabilized) IPW. addresses **baseline** confounding per-protocol analysis pipeline. Note: estimate time-varying treatment weights -treated analysis (Danaei 2013, Section 4.3). -treated analysis currently implemented. Robust standard errors within-person correlation handled downstream `survey::svydesign(ids = ~person_id_var)` `$irr()` `$km()` (Hernan 2008, Danaei 2013).

title: TTEEnrollment class for target trial emulation — TTEEnrollment
text: Fit weighted Poisson regression extract incidence rate ratios. Uses `survey::svyglm()` `quasipoisson` family person-level clustering (`ids = ~person_id_var`) robust standard errors. accounts within-person correlation across repeated trial entries (Hernan 2008, Danaei 2013). **IRR vs HR**: rare events (typical registry-based TTE studies), incidence rate ratio Poisson regression approximates hazard ratio Cox regression (Thompson 1977). Poisson model `splines::ns(tstop, df=3)` flexibly models baseline event rate follow-time — analogous Cox's nonparametric baseline hazard Danaei et al.'s "month follow-squared terms" pooled logistic regression. **Computational choice**: `quasipoisson` accounts overdispersion survey weights, `svyglm` scales large registry datasets (unlike `survey::svycoxph()`). computationally equivalent pooled logistic approach used Danaei et al. (2013). **Calendar-time adjustment**: `trial_id` present data (band-based enrollment), included model adjust calendar-time variation outcome rates across enrollment bands (Caniglia 2023, Danaei 2013). Uses natural splines >=5 unique trial IDs, linear term 2-4, omitted 1.

what: TTEPlan class for trial generation planning — TTEPlan
title: TTEPlan class for trial generation planning — TTEPlan
text: Bundles ETT grid, skeleton file paths, design column names single object using builder pattern. Create empty plan [TTEPlan$new()], add ETTs one time `$add_one_ett()`. Supports `plan[[]]` extract -th enrollment spec interactive testing. Design parameters (confounder_vars, person_id_var, treatment_var, etc.) stored per-ETT `ett` data.table, allowing different ETTs use different confounders design columns. Within enrollment_id (follow_up + age_group), design params must match.

title: TTEPlan class for trial generation planning — TTEPlan
text: max_follow_up (read-) maximum `follow_up` across ETTs.     Used `$enrollment_spec()` set `design$follow_up_time`     enrollment covers longest follow-per enrollment group.     Returns `NA` ETTs added.

title: TTEPlan class for trial generation planning — TTEPlan
text: `$add_one_ett(...)` Add one ETT row plan. Returns `invisible(self)`. `$save(dir)` Save plan disk `.qs2`. Returns `invisible(path)`. `$enrollment_spec()` Extract -th enrollment spec list design, age_range, etc. `$s1_generate_enrollments_and_ipw(...)` Run Loop 1: skeleton files trial panels + IPW. `$s2_generate_analysis_files_and_ipcw_pp(...)` Run Loop 2: per-ETT IPCW-PP + analysis file generation.

title: TTEPlan class for trial generation planning — TTEPlan
text: project_prefix Character, string used file naming. ett NULL data.table per-ETT columns. skeleton_files Character vector skeleton file paths. global_max_isoyearweek Admin censoring boundary. spec Parsed study spec ([tteplan_read_spec()]), NULL. expected_skeleton_file_count Expected number skeleton files, NULL. code_registry data.table [RegistryStudy]`$summary_table()`, NULL. expected_n_ids Total number individuals across batches, NULL. created_at POSIXct. plan created. registry_study_created_at POSIXct NULL. source RegistryStudy created. skeleton_created_at POSIXct NULL. skeleton files created (first file's attribute). period_width Integer, band width weeks enrollment (default: 4L). enrollment_counts Named list per-enrollment TARGET Item 8 data. element list : attrition Long-format data.table (trial_id, criterion,     n_persons, n_person_trials, n_intervention, n_comparator) showing     cumulative attrition eligibility step. Includes     "before_exclusions" row pre-filtering counts. matching data.table (trial_id, n_intervention_total,     n_comparator_total, n_intervention_enrolled, n_comparator_enrolled). output_dir Character. Directory enrollment/analysis files stored. results_enrollment Named list per-enrollment analysis results (keyed enrollment_id). results_ett Named list per-ETT analysis results (keyed ett_id). spec_reloaded_at POSIXct NULL. `$reload_spec()` last called refresh cosmetic labels. spec_reload_skipped_diffs Character vector structural spec differences `$reload_spec()` chose apply, NULL. spec_version Character. Spec version tag (e.g. `"v003"`) selects YAML filename results sub-directory. dir_tteplan_cp [CandidatePath] directory `tteplan.qs2` companion enrollment/analysis files live. dir_spec_cp [CandidatePath] directory containing spec YAML (`spec_vXXX.yaml`). dir_results_cp [CandidatePath] results base directory. `dir_results` (active binding) appends `spec_version` . registrystudy Embedded [RegistryStudy] R6 object. Owns rawbatch skeleton directory candidates; accessed via `plan$data_skeleton` `plan$data_rawbatch`. n_skeleton_files_limit Optional integer. non-NULL, `tteplan_load()` caps `self$skeleton_files` many entries refreshing `self$registrystudy`. Used dev configs want subset skeletons.

title: TTEPlan class for trial generation planning — TTEPlan
text: max_follow_up (read-) Maximum follow_up across ETTs. dir_tteplan (read-) Directory `tteplan.qs2` saved, resolved `self$dir_tteplan_cp` current host. dir_spec (read-) Directory containing spec YAML, resolved `self$dir_spec_cp`. dir_results_base (read-) Results base directory, resolved `self$dir_results_cp`. `dir_results` appends `spec_version`. dir_results (read-) Results directory version suffix: `file.path(self$dir_results_base, self$spec_version)`. tteplan (read-) Full path `tteplan.qs2`. spec_path (read-) Full path spec YAML (`spec_vXXX.yaml`) selected `self$spec_version`. spec_xlsx (read-) Full path `spec.xlsx` inside `self$dir_results`. tables_xlsx (read-) Full path `tables.xlsx` inside `self$dir_results`. data_skeleton (read-) Delegates `self$registrystudy$data_skeleton_dir`. data_rawbatch (read-) Delegates `self$registrystudy$data_rawbatch_dir`.

title: TTEPlan class for trial generation planning — TTEPlan
text: Loop 1: Create trial panels skeleton files compute IPW. Uses two-pass pipeline fix cross-batch matching ratio imbalance. Requires `self$spec` set (e.g., via [tteplan_from_spec_and_registrystudy()]). **Pass 1a (scout)**: Lightweight parallel pass reads     skeleton file, applies exclusions treatment, returns eligible     `(person_id, trial_id, intervention)` tuples. confounders enrollment. **Centralized matching**: Combines tuples batches,     per `trial_id` keeps intervention samples     `ratio * n_intervention` comparator globally. Stores counts     `self$enrollment_counts` TARGET Item 8 reporting. **Pass 1b (full enrollment)**: Parallel pass re-reads     skeleton file full processing (exclusions + confounders +     treatment), enrolls using pre-matched IDs (skipping per-batch     matching). Produces panel-expanded TTEEnrollment objects.

title: TTEPlan class for trial generation planning — TTEPlan

title: TTEPlan class for trial generation planning — TTEPlan
text: output_dir Optional directory override output files. `NULL` (default), uses `self$dir_tteplan`. impute_fn Imputation callback NULL (default: [tteenrollment_impute_confounders]). stabilize Logical, stabilize IPW (default: TRUE). n_workers Integer, concurrent subprocesses (default: 3L). swereg_dev_path Path local swereg dev copy, NULL. resume Logical. `TRUE`, skip enrollments whose `_imp_` file already exists `output_dir` (default: FALSE).

title: TTEPlan class for trial generation planning — TTEPlan
text: Loop 2: Per-ETT IPCW-PP calculation analysis file generation. ETT, loads imputed enrollment file, calls `$s4_prepare_for_analysis()` (outcome + IPCW-PP + weight combination + truncation), saves analysis-ready file.

title: TTEPlan class for trial generation planning — TTEPlan
text: output_dir Optional directory override containing imp files analysis files saved. `NULL` (default), uses `self$dir_tteplan`. estimate_ipcw_pp_separately_by_treatment Logical, estimate IPCW-PP separately treatment group (default: TRUE). estimate_ipcw_pp_with_gam Logical, use GAM IPCW-PP estimation (default: TRUE). n_workers Integer, concurrent subprocesses (default: 1L). swereg_dev_path Path local swereg dev copy, NULL. resume Logical. `TRUE`, skip ETTs whose analysis file already exists `output_dir` (default: FALSE).

title: TTEPlan class for trial generation planning — TTEPlan
text: Loop 3: Compute analysis results store plan. enrollment: loads one analysis file raw file, computes baseline characteristics (raw, unweighted, IPW, IPW truncated). ETT: loads analysis file, computes rates, IRR, heterogeneity test truncated untruncated weights. Results stored `self$results_enrollment` `self$results_ett`. Existing results skipped (resume-safe). Use `plan$save()` persist.

title: TTEPlan class for trial generation planning — TTEPlan

title: TTEPlan class for trial generation planning — TTEPlan
text: enrollment_ids Character vector enrollment IDs analyze, `NULL` (default) . ett_ids Character vector ETT IDs analyze, `NULL` (default) . output_dir Optional directory override. `NULL` (default), uses `self$dir_tteplan` (falls back legacy `self$output_dir` plans created CandidatePath migration). swereg_dev_path Path local swereg dev copy, NULL. force Logical (default `FALSE`). `TRUE`, drops cached results targeted scope recomputing. Scope follows `enrollment_ids` `ett_ids`: `NULL`, cached results cleared; otherwise matching entries dropped (untargeted enrollments / ETTs stay cached). Useful prior results produced broken environment (e.g. missing `survey` package -> 135 silent `skipped = TRUE` entries) want recompute without manually mutating `self$results_ett` / `self$results_enrollment`. n_workers Integer >= 1 (default `1L`). Number concurrent worker subprocesses enrollment loop per-ETT loop. worker reads analysis file fresh, peak RAM scales linearly `n_workers`; machines multi-GB analysis files, set conservatively. CPU threads per worker auto-partitioned `floor(detectCores() / n_workers)`.

Default: enrollment baseline observations. featured_etts Optional, either flat character vector ETT ids feature Forest plot, **named list** vectors. supplied named list, name becomes bold group header Forest plot (e.g. one group per treatment contrast). Defaults `self$output_dir`. forest_label_format Optional character(1) format string Forest plot row description. Supports `placeholder` tokens: `outcome_name`, `outcome_description`, `enrollment_name`, `enrollment_id`, `intervention_name`, `comparator_name`, `follow_up`, `ett_id`. `NULL` (default), uses `"outcome_name (follow_upw)"` grouped featured ETTs `"enrollment_name - outcome_name (follow_upw)"` otherwise. forest_desc_header Optional character(1) header label description column Forest plot left text panel. Defaults `"ETT"`.

title: Add cause of death data to skeleton — add_cods

title: Add cause of death data to skeleton — add_cods
code: add_cods(   skeleton,   dataset,   id_name,   cod_type = "both",   codes = list(icd10_F64_0 = c("F640"), icd10_F64_89 = c("F6489"), icd10_F64_089 =     c("F640", "F648", "F649")),   cods = NULL )

title: Add cause of death data to skeleton — add_cods
text: skeleton data.table containing main skeleton structure created create_skeleton dataset data.table containing death registry data cause death codes. Must columns person ID, death date (dodsdat), cause codes (ulorsak, morsak variables) id_name Character string specifying name ID variable dataset cod_type Character string specifying cause types search: "" (default) - Search underlying (ulorsak) multiple (morsak) causes "underlying" - Search underlying cause death (ulorsak) "multiple" - Search multiple/contributing causes (morsak variables) codes Named list ICD-10 code patterns. Names become column   names skeleton; values character vectors code prefixes. Matching **prefix-** via startsWith(). pattern like   "I21" matches "I21", "I210", "I219", etc.   regex – characters ^, $, *,   [-Z] taken literally match anything. Prefixing pattern "!" turns *row-level veto*:   source row whose code matches (un-prefixed) pattern masked   contribute. veto applied per source row   across scanned columns (e.g. ulorsak + morsak*),   reset code names. Important: veto operates   raw source row, (id, isoyearweek) bucket – person   vetoed code non-vetoed code week,   non-vetoed code still triggers TRUE week. Examples: c("I21", "I22") – acute MI code. c("", "!I21", "!I22") – chapter-code except       acute MI. cods Deprecated alias codes. supplied, warning issued value used codes.

title: Add diagnosis data to skeleton — add_diagnoses
text: skeleton data.table containing main skeleton structure created create_skeleton dataset data.table containing hospital registry data diagnosis codes. Must columns person ID, admission date (indatum), least one diagnosis code column. Expected diagnosis columns make_lowercase_names(): hdia (main), dia1/dia2/etc (secondary), ekod1/ekod2/etc (external causes), icd7*, icd9* id_name Character string specifying name ID variable dataset diag_type Character string specifying diagnosis types search: "" (default) - Search main (hdia), secondary (dia*),     external cause (ekod*), historical ICD version columns (icd7*, icd9*) "main" - Search main diagnosis column (hdia) codes Named list ICD code patterns. Names become column   names skeleton; values character vectors code prefixes. Matching **prefix-** via startsWith(). pattern like   "F32" matches "F32", "F320", "F321", etc.   regex – characters ^, $, *,   [-Z] taken literally match anything. Prefixing pattern "!" turns *row-level veto*:   source row whose code matches (un-prefixed) pattern masked   contribute. veto applies per source row across   scanned columns (hdia + dia* + ekod* + ...),   reset code names. Important: veto operates   raw source row, (id, isoyearweek) bucket – person   vetoed code non-vetoed code week,   non-vetoed code still triggers TRUE week. Examples: list(depression = c("F32", "F33")) – depression code. list(f64_minus_640 = c("F64", "!F640")) – F64*       code except literal F640. diags Deprecated. Use codes instead.

title: Add diagnosis data to skeleton — add_diagnoses
text: skeleton data.table modified reference diagnosis variables added.   New boolean variables created diagnosis pattern, TRUE diagnosis present.

title: Add diagnosis data to skeleton — add_diagnoses
text: function searches across different diagnosis code column types based diag_type parameter: diag_type = "": Searches hdia (main diagnosis),     dia1, dia2, ... (secondary diagnoses), ekod1, ekod2, ...     (external cause codes), icd7* (ICD-7 codes), icd9* (ICD-9 codes) diag_type = "main": Searches hdia (main diagnosis) ## Filtering source (inpatient/outpatient/cancer) diagnosis dataset must contain source column valid values ("inpatient", "outpatient", "cancer"). track diagnoses separately source, filter dataset calling function:
code: # Inpatient diagnoses only inpatient_data <- diagnoses[source == "inpatient"] add_diagnoses(skeleton, inpatient_data, "lopnr", diags = list(   "depression_inpatient" = c("F32", "F33") ))  # Outpatient diagnoses only outpatient_data <- diagnoses[source == "outpatient"] add_diagnoses(skeleton, outpatient_data, "lopnr", diags = list(   "depression_outpatient" = c("F32", "F33") ))  # Combined (any source) - default behavior add_diagnoses(skeleton, diagnoses, "lopnr", diags = list(   "depression_any" = c("F32", "F33") ))

Consider adding them for automatic date parsing.  # Create skeleton skeleton <- create_skeleton(fake_person_ids[1:10], "2020-01-01", "2020-12-31")  # Add diagnoses diag_patterns <- list(   "depression" = c("F32", "F33"),   "anxiety" = c("F40", "F41") ) add_diagnoses(skeleton, fake_diagnoses, "lopnr", "both", diag_patterns)

Consider adding them for automatic date parsing.  # Create skeleton skeleton <- create_skeleton(fake_person_ids[1:10], "2020-01-01", "2020-12-31")  # Add ICD-O-3 codes for specific cancer types cancer_codes <- list(   "adenocarcinoma" = c("^8140"),   "breast_cancer" = c("^C50") ) add_icdo3s(skeleton, fake_diagnoses, "lopnr", cancer_codes)

what: Add quality registry data to skeleton — add_quality_registry
title: Add quality registry data to skeleton — add_quality_registry
text: Creates boolean skeleton columns filter expressions evaluated quality registry data (e.g., Riksstroke, NKBC). Unlike [add_diagnoses()] matches ICD code prefixes, function evaluates arbitrary R expressions dataset columns.

title: Add quality registry data to skeleton — add_quality_registry
code: add_quality_registry(skeleton, dataset, id_name, date_col, codes = list())

title: Add quality registry data to skeleton — add_quality_registry
text: skeleton data.table containing main skeleton structure created create_skeleton. dataset data.table containing quality registry data person ID date column. id_name Character string specifying name ID variable dataset. date_col Character string specifying name date column dataset (must Date IDate class). codes Named list filter definitions. Names become variable names skeleton. Values either TRUE (event flag) quote() expressions evaluated dataset columns. Example: list("stroke_event" = TRUE, "stroke_tia" = quote(tia == 1))

title: Add quality registry data to skeleton — add_quality_registry
text: skeleton data.table modified reference boolean   variables added. New columns FALSE non-event weeks,   FALSE filter expressions evaluate NA (missing data).

title: Add quality registry data to skeleton — add_quality_registry
text: entry codes produces one boolean column skeleton: TRUE: event indicator — TRUE week registry event quote(expr): filter expression evaluated dataset columns.     TRUE expression evaluates TRUE, FALSE otherwise (including NA). Multiple events person-week aggregated ().

title: Add quality registry data to skeleton — add_quality_registry
code: # Create fake data data("fake_person_ids", package = "swereg") skeleton <- create_skeleton(fake_person_ids[1:5], "2020-01-01", "2020-12-31")  fake_registry <- data.table::data.table(   lopnr = c(fake_person_ids[1], fake_person_ids[2]),   event_date = as.Date(c("2020-03-15", "2020-06-20")),   severity = c(5, 18),   treated = c(1, 2) )  add_quality_registry(skeleton, fake_registry, "lopnr",   date_col = "event_date",   codes = list(     "reg_event" = TRUE,     "reg_severe" = quote(severity >= 15),     "reg_treated" = quote(treated == 1)   ) )

what: Add prescription drug data to skeleton — add_rx
title: Add prescription drug data to skeleton — add_rx
text: Searches specific drug codes (ATC product names) Swedish prescription registry data adds corresponding boolean variables skeleton based prescription periods duration treatment.

title: Add prescription drug data to skeleton — add_rx
code: add_rx(   skeleton,   lmed,   id_name = "lopnr",   codes = list(rx_hormones_pubblock = c("L02AE", "H01CA")),   source = "atc",   rxs = NULL )

title: Add prescription drug data to skeleton — add_rx
text: skeleton data.table containing main skeleton structure created create_skeleton lmed data.table containing prescription registry data (LMED). Must columns person ID, prescription date (edatum), treatment duration (fddd), drug codes (atc) product names (produkt) id_name Character string specifying name ID variable (default: "lopnr") codes Named list drug code patterns. Names become column   names skeleton; values character vectors. Matching   semantics depend source (see ). Prefixing pattern "!" turns *row-level   veto*: prescription whose code matches (un-prefixed)   pattern masked contribute named output   column. Final rule: prescription row contributes named   column iff least one un-prefixed pattern matches   "!" pattern matches. Behaviour notes worth knowing: Vetoes independent per named code.       "!" entry inside one list element leak       element codes list. Two named       codes can produce two completely different views       prescription rows. Veto match style follows source.       source = "atc" veto prefix-based via       startsWith(): "!N05AA" masks N05AA01,       N05AA02, ... source = "produkt" veto       exact-match via %chin%: "!Sertralin"       mask "Sertralin Sandoz" product       names exact, prefixes. -negative pattern set produces empty       column. c("!N05AA") gives -FALSE       result – without positive pattern set       carve . Use wider include + negative, e.g.       c("N05A", "!N05AA"). Per-(id, isoyearweek) aggregation respects       veto per-source-row basis. veto removes specific       prescription rows matched set per-week       aggregation runs. person vetoed Rx       non-vetoed Rx whose coverage windows overlap       skeleton week, non-vetoed Rx still drives week       TRUE – veto kills row's contribution,       whole week. Examples: c("N06A") – antidepressant. c("N05A", "!N05AA", "!N05AB") – antipsychotic       except first-generation typical agents. Default includes hormone therapy codes puberty blockers   (L02AE, H01CA). Common patterns include: Antidepressants: "N06A" Hormone therapy: "G03", "L02AE", "H01CA" Cardiovascular drugs: "C07", "C08", "C09" source Character string specifying search field matching strategy: "atc" (default) - Prefix matching ATC codes (e.g., "N06A" matches "N06AB06").     Uses startsWith() fast C-level matching. "produkt" - Exact matching product names (e.g., "Delestrogen" matches     "Delestrogen", "Delestrogen Extra"). Uses %chin% fast lookup. rxs Deprecated. Use codes instead.

title: Transform a row-dependent variable to a row-independent variable using first occurrence — make_rowind_first_occurrence
text: swereg distinguishes two variable shapes longitudinal skeleton data: **row-dependent** (prefix `rd_`) Values can change time     person. Examples: `rd_age_continuous`, `rd_education`,     `rd_income_inflation_adjusted`. **row-independent** (prefix `ri_`) Values fixed     person-level. Examples: `ri_birthcountry`, `ri_age_first_diagnosis`,     `ri_isoyear_first_diagnosis`, `ri_register_tag`. function automates common `rd_` -> `ri_` transformation capturing "value first time something became true". transformation follows steps: 1. Create temporary column `condition` TRUE 2. Use `first_non_na()` find first occurrence person 3. Clean temporary column automatically Equivalent manual pattern: dt[condition, temp := value_var] dt[, new_var := first_non_na(temp), = .(id)] dt[, temp := NULL]

title: Transform a row-dependent variable to a row-independent variable using first occurrence — make_rowind_first_occurrence

what: Run a function on each work item in parallel via processx — parallel_pool
title: Run a function on each work item in parallel via processx — parallel_pool
text: Dispatches items across `n_workers` concurrent R subprocesses. item's arguments saved qs2 tempfile passed standalone worker R script (`inst/`) via command-line arguments. Results read back qs2 output tempfiles. avoids R's IPC serialization overhead entirely.

title: Run a function on each work item in parallel via processx — parallel_pool
code: parallel_pool(   items,   worker_script,   n_workers,   swereg_dev_path = NULL,   p = NULL,   collect = TRUE,   ... )

title: Run a function on each work item in parallel via processx — parallel_pool
text: items List argument lists, one per work item. element saved qs2 tempfile read worker script. worker_script Basename worker R script `inst/` (e.g. `"worker_s1a.R"`). n_workers Integer number concurrent subprocesses. swereg_dev_path Path local swereg dev copy (`devtools::load_all()`), `NULL` use installed swereg. p Progressor function [progressr::progressor()], `NULL`. Called per completed work item main process. collect `TRUE` (default), collect return worker results output tempfiles. `FALSE`, discard (useful workers save output directly final location). ... Ignored (absorbs unused arguments callers).

title: Run a function on each work item in parallel via processx — parallel_pool

title: Parse Swedish registry dates — parse_swedish_date
text: date_string Character vector dates Swedish registry format default_month_day Character string default month-day year provided (default: "0701" July 1st) default_day Character string default day year-month provided (default: "15" 15th) na_strings Character vector strings treat NA (default: c("", "NA", "9999", "99999999"))

title: Parse Swedish registry dates — parse_swedish_date
text: Date vector parsed dates

title: Parse Swedish registry dates — parse_swedish_date
text: Swedish registry dates often come different formats: 4 characters (YYYY): year known - adds default_month_day 6 characters (YYYYMM): Year month known - adds default_day 8 characters (YYYYMMDD): Full date known - uses -Special handling unknown date components: "0000" "9999" endings (unknown month+day) replaced default_month_day "00" "99" endings (unknown day) replaced default_day Invalid dates return NA warnings "99" convention used quality registries (e.g., Riksstroke) indicate unknown date components, "00" common convention.

title: Install a progressr handler that works in interactive R and RStudio jobs — setup_progress_handlers
text: * **Interactive sessions** (normal R console, RStudio foreground console):   single-line carriage-return repaint `clear = TRUE`. behavior   normal terminal progress bar – updates place, disappears   run finishes. * **Non-interactive sessions** (RStudio background jobs spawned via   *Source Background Job* / `rstudioapi::jobRunScript()`, Rscript, CI):   append trailing newline `clear = FALSE`. step becomes new   line log finished bars stay scrollback. Carriage   returns (job logs honor) never emitted. Also forces `options("progressr.enable" = TRUE)` progressr emits signals non-interactive sessions – without , every `progressor()` emission silently dropped jobRunScript subprocess bar ever appears.

title: Create a TTEPlan from a study specification — tteplan_from_spec_and_registrystudy
text: study [RegistryStudy] R6 object, typically loaded via [registrystudy_load()]. Owns rawbatch skeleton path candidates. candidate_dir_spec Character vector candidate directories contain spec YAML `spec_vXXX.yaml`. first existing directory used locate spec. candidate_dir_tteplan Character vector candidate directories `tteplan.qs2` lives (created `plan$save()`). candidate_dir_results Character vector candidate directories results BASE directory (without version suffix – plan appends `spec_version` internally). spec_version Optional character scalar like `"v003"` selecting spec YAML. `NULL`, read `spec$study$implementation$version`. project_id Optional character scalar display/logging. `NULL`, read `spec$study$implementation$project_prefix`. n_skeleton_files Optional integer: NULL, first `n_skeleton_files` files used (faster dev iterations). Stored plan `n_skeleton_files_limit` [tteplan_load()] can re-apply host transfer. global_max_isoyearweek Administrative censoring boundary (isoyearweek string, e.g., "2023-52"). `NULL` (default), auto-detected `max(isoyearweek)` first skeleton file. Also runs [tteplan_validate_spec()] skeleton. period_width Integer, band width weeks enrollment time aggregation (default: 4L). Stored plan passed TTEDesign.
