title: swereg 26.5.17
text: RegistryStudy$compute_population() RegistryStudy$compute_summary() longer public methods. now internal run automatically end $process_skeletons(). Population computation declarative: pass population_by_specs RegistryStudy$new() (list character vectors, one per desired aggregation). registered spec pre-computed per-batch meta_*.qs2 sidecar reduced population_<spec>.qs2 per process_skeletons() run. New getter study$population(= c(...)) reads cached population table. Errors clear message population_by_specs. New active binding study$summary reads back summary.qs2.

title: swereg 26.5.17
text: study$population(= ...) now sub-second meta-walk instead re-loading every full skeleton disk call. First call process_skeletons() fast per-batch aggregations computed -memory skeletons already loaded. Adding new spec population_by_specs runs triggers meta-refresh next $process_skeletons(): skeletons disk rewritten; meta_*.qs2 sidecars augmented missing aggregation.

title: swereg 26.5.17
text: $delete_skeletons() now also removes cached population_*.qs2 summary.qs2 files data_skeleton_dir.

title: swereg 26.5.17
text: Excel excel_spec_summary() renderer: named criteria enrollment’s additional_inclusion / additional_exclusion blocks now render one indent deeper parent section header (previously collided ), Age range: row bolded like criterion names. Adds third indent level (sub-sub) new styles st_*_sub_sub_item / st_*_sub_sub_label, new add_sub_sub_item() helper, sub_sub argument add_kv() / add_yellow() / add_var() / add_derived_var(). Excel excel_spec_summary() renderer: surfaces spec$standing_methods$calendar_time first entry Confounders section present. Calendar time trial registration auto-adjusted via IPW/IPCW models, rendering explicitly stops protocol reviewers re-asking “calendar year?” every TTE.

title: swereg 26.5.16
text: status.txt bucket lines now carry denominators – e.g. dorsu  90 / 137 shows 90 137 dorsu_* variables never matched. denominator appears per-bucket header (dorsu (90 / 137):). Makes “prefix unusually problematic just big?” readable glance.

title: swereg 26.5.15
text: status.txt: section (never-matched, rare) now leads bucket-count summary (one row per registry prefix, descending size) per-bucket detail blocks. Per-bucket lists never collapsed – every column name appears full. Rare cutoff rendered “1-9” (clearer “< 10”).

title: swereg 26.5.14
text: [ok] count appears first (noise) headline number (“many variables look healthy”) immediately readable. Never-matched rare-variable sections grouped column’s registry-type prefix (everything first underscore: dorsm, sv, os, osd, can, op, rx, …), sorted bucket size descending dominant problem shows first. Avoids 252-line flat alphabetical list previously forced reader scroll past entire never-matched dump reach actually-OK count. numbers comma-formatted (8,852,776 8852776). Trailing pointer summary.qs2 full per-column detail.

title: swereg 26.5.13
text: Meta sidecar now carries weekly/annual splits date range weekly data: n_rows_weekly / n_rows_annual, n_persons_weekly / n_persons_annual, weekly_min_isoyearweek / weekly_max_isoyearweek, annual_min_isoyear / annual_max_isoyear. Per-column $counts now emit n_person_weeks_with (TRUE rows is_isoyear == FALSE) n_person_years_with (TRUE rows is_isoyear == TRUE) separately; previously single field misleadingly named since actually nrow() (weekly + annual combined). $compute_summary() aggregates splits; status.txt now prints WEEKLY ANNUAL time periods respective denominators. TSV audit-track gains n_person_years_with column header comments periods.

title: swereg 26.5.12
text: Removed per-batch code-check warning machinery added PR #4 + 26.5.10. Specifically removed: start_code_check_session() / end_code_check_session() exports, warn_unmatched_codes() / warn_empty_logical_cols() exports, internal .swereg_codes_pre() / .swereg_codes_post() hooks, .code_check_snapshot() / .code_check_merge() / .code_check_emit(), code_check_state field meta sidecar. Rationale: information now exposed usefully via RegistryStudy$compute_summary() (see ), two parallel mechanisms reporting data redundant. RegistryStudy$save_skeleton() longer takes code_check_state argument (callers). meta sidecar shape changed: code_check_state dropped, n_persons added, every entry applied_registry now carries $counts sub-field. Older meta sidecars (built version) keep working $counts field missing, $compute_summary() report zero per-column counts batches. fix, delete affected skeleton_*.qs2 + meta_*.qs2 files (call study$delete_skeletons()) re-run $process_skeletons().

title: swereg 26.5.12
text: RegistryStudy$compute_summary(): aggregates per-batch meta_*.qs2 sidecars study-wide sanity report. Always writes summary.qs2 (binary; programmatic reload) status.txt (human-readable flag report) data_skeleton_dir. full runs (every expected batch present), also writes git-tracked summary_<UTC>_<git-sha>_<swereg-ver>.tsv new data_summaries_dir candidate, counts suppress_below (default 5) masked "<N" (Swedish registry data convention). Partial runs explicitly skip TSV. New data_summaries_dir constructor parameter (optional). Defaults NULL; NULL, $compute_summary() skips TSV even full runs. Skeleton$apply_code_entry() now computes per-column n_persons_with n_person_weeks_with every column adds, stored entry’s applied_registry record. primitive $compute_summary() rolls . kept – expand_codes() / expand_code_list() (bracket / range expansion utilities PR #4) remain. expansion code patterns happens inline add_*() function; removed pieces specifically per-call warning emission machinery, code expansion machinery.

title: swereg 26.5.11
text: .swereg_dev_path() (helper hands package root callr workers can devtools::load_all() dev source parent) returning system.file(package="swereg") directly, dev-loaded package inst/ subdirectory – package root devtools::load_all() expects. Workers loaded broken namespace $process_skeletons() failed batch 1 attempt apply non-function. Now strips trailing /inst worker receives actual package root.

title: swereg 26.5.10
text: Skeleton schema bumped version 4 version 5 introduce meta_%05d.qs2 sidecar next every skeleton_%05d.qs2. Existing skeleton directories earlier versions readable; run study$delete_skeletons() re-run $process_skeletons() regenerate. sidecar small (KB) lets $process_skeletons() incremental-rebuild check reading meta – avoiding full skeleton deserialise common -change path. 2000-batch delivery 500 MB skeletons turns ~1 TB read MB.

title: swereg 26.5.10
text: RegistryStudy$process_skeletons() now emits single consolidated code-check warning end every run, covering every batch scope. Sequential parallel runs behave identically per-batch accumulators flow meta sidecars disk rather via -memory state. RegistryStudy$skeleton_pipeline_hashes() now reads small meta sidecars instead deserialising every skeleton, transparent fallback loading skeleton meta missing. Significantly faster large studies.

title: swereg 26.5.10
text: code-check session machinery introduced 26.5.9 now fully internal: previously-exported start_code_check_session(), end_code_check_session(), expand_codes(), expand_code_list(), warn_unmatched_codes(), warn_empty_logical_cols() longer exported. Users running $process_skeletons() get cross-batch aggregation automatically; users running manual loops outside RegistryStudy get per-call warnings (pre-26.5.9 behaviour). need cross-batch aggregation manual loop, open issue ’ll re-export.

title: swereg 26.5.9
text: add_diagnoses(), add_operations(), add_cods(), add_rx(), add_icdo3s(), add_snomed3s() add_snomedo10s() now accept bracket / character-class / range patterns directly (e.g. "I2[0-5]", "FN[ABCDEGW][0-9][0-9]", "!302[-Z]"). Bracket expansion runs unconditionally; matchers continue use startsWith() literal prefixes. add_* family now runs pre-call (per-literal source-data) post-call (column-level) sanity checks automatically. Bad patterns surface run-time instead producing silent empty columns. checks can disabled via options(swereg.check_codes = FALSE). pre-call check also runs cheap, data-free syntax check expanded code list, firing milliseconds first add_*() call rather hour 6 multi-hour batched pipeline. warns expanded literal empty contains regex metacharacters (^ $ * + ? . ( ) | \ [ ]) match startsWith(). Skipped automatically add_rx(source = "produkt") product names exact-matched via %chin% may legitimately contain characters. Contributed @alexengberg (PR #4).

title: swereg 26.5.8
text: File-naming format width bumped 3 digits 5 digits batch / ETT identifiers. Rawbatch files become 00001_rawbatch_lmed.qs2 (001_rawbatch_lmed.qs2); skeleton files become skeleton_00001.qs2 (skeleton_001.qs2); ETT identifiers become ETT00001 (ETT001). Existing -disk files using old 3-digit width recognised new code – callers must rename via shell regenerate. Affects RegistryStudy (rawbatch + skeleton) TTEPlan (ett_id, file_analysis = "<prefix>_analysis_<ett_id>.qs2" derived name).

title: swereg 26.5.7
text: registrystudy_load(): parameter renamed candidate_dir_rawbatch -> candidate_dir_meta. Callers must update passed name. Behaviour unchanged callers passed path used RegistryStudy$new(data_meta_dir = ...) (defaults rawbatch dir, existing scripts continue work positionally).

title: swereg 26.5.7
text: RegistryStudy$new() gains data_meta_dir: candidate paths directory holding registrystudy.qs2. Defaults data_rawbatch_dir (full backward compatibility). Pass explicit value – e.g. parent rawbatch – keep singleton control file per-batch data directory. Exposed read-active binding. $meta_file now resolves file.path(self$data_meta_dir, "registrystudy.qs2").

title: swereg 26.5.6
text: create_skeleton() now ~8x faster uses ~7.5x less memory every cohort size tested (1k–25k IDs 11-year range; linear scaling). win comes sorting single time spine replicating per id, instead expand.grid()-ing full cartesian product sorting result. Output identical previous implementation modulo hidden data.table secondary index attribute. Contributed @gkaramanis (PR #3).

title: swereg 26.5.6
text: New dev/bench/ scaffold tracking performance regressions: Rscript dev/bench/run_all.R runs benchmarks current source, Rscript dev/bench/diff.R compares run checked-baseline.csv exits non-zero >20% time / >50% memory regressions. Excluded package build via .Rbuildignore. New tests/testthat/test-create_skeleton-parity.R (31 tests) pinning create_skeleton() output embedded reference oracle plus structural invariants edge cases (empty / duplicate / NA ids, single-day range, ISO W53 year, year boundary).

title: swereg 26.4.28
text: RegistryStudy$process_skeletons() now fails fast batch error instead swallowing warning() pushing remaining batches. pipelines run unattended days; batch 1 fails 10 minutes (e.g. systematic bug, missing column, unreadable rawbatch file), user wants SSH within minutes see failure – end 4-day run remaining batches failing root cause. failing batch’s underlying error surfaced via stop() includes () batch number failed, (b) original error message, (c) hint successful batches persisted disk user can rerun batches = ... retry failed one. n_workers > 1 (callr subprocess) path, -flight workers killed via kill_tree() stop() don’t keep burning compute likely systematic failure. Callers intentionally want old “complete warnings” behaviour can wrap call tryCatch(error = function(e) ...).

title: swereg 26.4.28
text: add_rx() now supports "!"-prefixed exclusion patterns, restoring parity add_diagnoses() family. Previously, add_rx’s matcher one-shot Reduce(|, ...) union per-pattern branching, "!"-prefixed entries silently treated literal first characters ATC code (never match) literal product names (, accidentally, include brand named leading !). Now: matches antipsychotic except first-generation (N05AA / N05AB) classes – closing spec-expressiveness gap previously forced exhaustive enumeration every desired sub-code. veto independent per named code (leak across list entries) applies whether source = "atc" (prefix match) source = "produkt" (exact match).
code: codes = list(rx_n05_atypical = c("N05A", "!N05AA", "!N05AB"))

title: swereg 26.4.28
text: Expanded add_rx() @param codes spell four nuances add_diagnoses family carried implicitly: vetoes independent per named code (leak across list entries); veto match style follows source (prefix atc, exact produkt – "!Sertralin" mask "Sertralin Sandoz"); -negative pattern sets produce empty columns; per- source-row veto interacts per-week aggregation non-vetoed Rx still drives week TRUE even vetoed Rx overlaps week. Fixed misleading pattern-syntax documentation add_diagnoses(), add_cods(), add_icdo3s(), add_snomed3s(), add_snomedo10s(), add_operations(). previous text described patterns regex auto-prepended ^ anchors example strings like "^F640" / "^8140" / "^80146002". actual matcher prefix-via startsWith() – literal ^ pattern treated ordinary character silently matches nothing. Updated function’s @param codes describe real contract (prefix matching, regex), added clear explanation "!"-prefixed row-level vetoes (including important detail veto operates raw source row, (id, isoyearweek) bucket – non-vetoed code week still triggers TRUE). add_rx() documentation now explicitly notes support "!" exclusion patterns (matcher simple union).

title: swereg 26.4.27
text: Renamed user-facing “exposure” terminology results workbook match project’s TTE vocabulary, assignment treatment active arm intervention. Sensitivity sheet’s identifier column header Exposure now Intervention, per-arm measurement columns Events (exp), PY (exp), Rate/100k (exp) now Events (int), PY (int), Rate/100k (int) (paired unchanged (cmp) columns). Existing workbooks already disk rewritten – re-run plan$export_tables() regenerate.

title: swereg 26.4.27
text: TTEPlan$s3_analyze() gains force argument (default FALSE). TRUE, cached results_enrollment results_ett entries targeted scope dropped recomputation. Scope follows enrollment_ids / ett_ids: NULL, cached results cleared; otherwise matching entries dropped. Provides supported way recompute broken environment produced skipped = TRUE placeholders (e.g. missing survey package), without poking R6 internals calling script. TTEPlan$s3_analyze() gains n_workers argument (default 1L). enrollment loop per-ETT loop now dispatch parallel_pool() requested concurrency (previously hardcoded 1L call sites). subprocess loads analysis file, peak RAM scales linearly n_workers; CPU threads per worker auto-partitioned floor(detectCores() / n_workers).

title: swereg 26.4.27
text: CONSORT cascade longer double-counts global cohort. .s1_compute_attrition(), per-trial summary tables (before_row per-criterion rows[[]]) grouped trial_id pt0 included person-weeks outside trial period. rows collapsed spurious (trial_id = NA, criterion) group whose n_persons later got summed together legitimate before_global / global_rows[[]] row per-batch aggregation (= .(trial_id, criterion) centralized matching step), roughly doubling global cohort number CONSORT diagram attrition tables. Per-trial counts arm counts unaffected. Fix filters pt0[!.na(trial_id)] (pt_i[!.na(trial_id)]) computing per-trial summaries; global row still computed unfiltered pt0 captures full pre-exclusion uniqueN. Existing enrollment_counts_*.qs2 files need regenerated re-running s1 pick corrected counts. inst/worker_s2.R now passes sep_by_tx (matching .s2_worker() plan-side item field) instead stale sep_by_exp. Previously plan$s2_generate_analysis_files_and_ipcw_pp() failed first ETT unused argument (sep_by_exp = params$sep_by_exp).

title: swereg 26.4.27
text: Promoted runtime-required packages Suggests Imports install automatically: survey, survival, mgcv, MASS, scales, glue, openxlsx, patchwork, DiagrammeR, DiagrammeRsvg, rsvg. referenced unconditionally R/ (requireNamespace() guards alternative code paths). Previously, missing survey caused every IRR call s3_analyze silently captured list(skipped = TRUE, reason = "package called 'survey'"), produced empty forest plot export_tables visible error. Missing DiagrammeR/DiagrammeRsvg/rsvg similarly skipped CONSORT sidecars warning.

title: swereg 26.4.25
text: Version bump.

title: swereg 26.4.22
text: .s1_compute_attrition() emits global trial_id = NA row per criterion alongside existing per-trial rows, true uniqueN(person_id) across whole skeleton. Summing per-trial n_persons -counted anyone entering one trial; global row honest number. Per-trial rows retained diagnostic slicing. .build_consort_dot() now renders one lumped red side-box bulleted list every exclusion criterion (CONSORT-2010 convention) instead stacked red box per criterion, box reports N persons / M person-trials. Enrollment titles split ” (” onto two lines long spec labels don’t blow box width. TTEEnrollment$rates() gains n_persons column per treatment arm, using design$person_id_var. Results workbook: enrollment’s combined-baseline sheet opens one-line “Cohort: N persons contributed M sequential trial enrollments” summary. companion Attrition_{id} sheet carries tabular form CONSORT numbers (criterion / n_persons / n_person_trials / excluded counts / n_intervention / n_comparator) reviewers can cite exact figures without measuring pixels PNG sidecar. $register_codes() auto-validates add_* contract. Every call registered fn now wrapped pre/post-state check: row count must preserved, structural columns (id, isoyear, isoyearweek, is_isoyear) must still present, every column name registration’s codes list must actually exist skeleton call. Contract violations error loudly pointer back offending $register_codes(<label>) entry. Custom add_* functions plugged pipeline (Norwegian registries, regional Swedish cohorts, payer claims, …) get enforcement free; built-ins already pass checks, behaviour change existing registrations.

title: swereg 26.4.22
text: New vignette builtin-add-functions — end--end walkthrough every add_* function swereg ships (add_onetime, add_annual, add_diagnoses, add_operations, add_rx, add_cods, add_quality_registry), pattern syntax, collision policies, typical end--end ordering. New vignette custom-add-functions — write add_* function registries swereg doesn’t ship support (non-Swedish registries, -house data, quality registries without dedicated built-ins). Covers contract, reusing built-ins via $register_codes(fn = swereg::add_diagnoses), fn_args extra knobs like diag_type = "main", complete add_vaccinations() worked example run real RegistryStudy, demonstration auto-wrap firing deliberately broken function, design cheat sheet lessons built-ins.

title: swereg 26.4.22
text: RegistryStudy$skeleton_pipeline_hashes() now reports progress. last batch finishes, $process_skeletons() calls $write_pipeline_snapshot(), turn calls $skeleton_pipeline_hashes() collect per-batch metadata. function deserialize every skeleton_*.qs2 file disk (via qs2::qs_read()) read handful hash fields, many/large batches can take tens minutes. Previously ran silently, making $process_skeletons() look hung dispatching last batch. now prints explanatory message front ticks progressr bar per file.

title: swereg 26.4.16
text: Consolidated hand-rolled vignettes 3-step (skeleton1-create, skeleton2-clean, skeleton3-analyze) 2-step manual workflow (skeleton-create, skeleton-analyze) matches actual architecture. Renamed pkgdown “Hand-rolled” section “Manual workflow”. Updated skeleton-concept, skeleton-pipeline, CLAUDE.md reflect 2-step model.

title: swereg 26.4.20
text: add_cods() now accepts codes = like sibling add_* functions (previously cods =). old cods = name kept deprecated alias warning. mismatch caused every add_cods entry registered via RegistryStudy$register_codes() fail silently "unused argument (codes = list(...))" — dispatcher always passes codes = ... add_cods one expecting name. RegistryStudy$process_skeletons() progress bar now advances batches fail. Previously, parallel branch’s error handler emitted warning never ticked progressor, single failing batch left bar frozen — combined options("warn") default 0 buffering warnings function returns — produced symptom “progress bar stuck 0% indefinitely”. bar now ticks success failure, failed-batch messages show bar’s (last: ...) slot FAILED tag. Warnings also emitted immediate. = TRUE failures surface real time regardless session’s warn setting. serial branch gained matching error handling (previously single batch error aborted whole run).

title: swereg 26.4.20
text: TTE vocabulary rename (exposure / exposed / unexposed → treatment / intervention / comparator): TTE system now uses PICO-aligned terminology. treatment umbrella concept (variable naming assignment); intervention comparator two arm values. matches TTE papers written major medical journals (BMJ, NEJM) Hernán’s canonical target-trial references. Migration: YAML spec: exposure: → treatment: exposed_value: → intervention_value: arms.exposed: → arms.intervention: TTEDesign$new() arguments: exposure_var = ... → treatment_var = ... time_exposure_var = ... → time_treatment_var = ... Skeleton / trial-panel columns produced pipeline: baseline_exposed → baseline_intervention rd_exposed → rd_intervention n_exposed / n_unexposed (CONSORT / matching summaries) → n_intervention / n_comparator (_total / _enrolled variants) Logical semantics unchanged: TRUE = intervention arm, FALSE = comparator arm. Column names now describe TRUE means, clearer prior “exposed” (ambiguous assignment time-varying status). User-chosen column names (e.g., column named "exposed" user data) unaffected — rename touches package-provided API surface.

title: swereg 26.4.20
text: RegistryStudy$register_derived_codes(codes, , ): registers “derived” code entry doesn’t read rawbatch data, instead ORs together already-existing skeleton columns earlier primary entries. nm codes, writes <>_<nm> := <[1]>_<nm> | <[2]>_<nm> | .... Use case: building combined outcome columns like osd_* = os_* | dorsu_* | dorsm_* hospital half comes add_diagnoses OV+SV death half comes add_cods DORS – two functions can’t share single combine_as argument search different raw-data columns (hdia/dia* vs ulorsak/morsak*). Derived entries full first-class citizens code registry: get fingerprint, participate per-entry incremental sync via Skeleton$sync_with_registry(), fold upstream primary fingerprints editing upstream primary’s fn_args / groups / codes (e.g. flipping cod_type "underlying" "multiple") automatically cascades derived replay. Derived entries must registered upstream primaries (apply runs registration order).

title: swereg 26.4.19
text: New Skeleton R6 class: per-batch skeleton files disk now serialized Skeleton R6 objects instead bare data.tables. carry phase provenance (framework hash, applied code registry fingerprints keyed minimal descriptor, ordered named list applied phase-3 “randvars” steps). Legacy bare-data.table skeleton files auto-wrapped first load backwards compatibility. Downstream code reads skeletons via qs2_read() now needs unwrap via sk$data; three swereg internal call sites (.s1_prepare_skeleton(), .s1b_worker() cache reuse, tteplan_from_spec_and_registrystudy()) updated. RegistryStudy$process_skeletons() signature changed: drops process_fn callback argument. Callers must pre-register pipeline functions via $register_framework(fn) $register_randvars(name, fn) calling $process_skeletons(). new three-phase orchestration re-runs phase relevant part pipeline changed: Phase 1 (framework): full rebuild function body/formals hash change. Phase 3 (randvars): ordered per-step divergence-point rewind-- replay. Editing one step replays step everything downstream ; upstream steps untouched. Phase 2 (codes): per-entry fingerprint diff. Adding/removing one code entry touches entry’s columns existing skeletons. typical “edit one ICD-10 code” workflow, drops re-run cost full pipeline rebuild roughly one-Nth phase 2, N number registered code entries. RegistryStudy schema bump (3 → 4): existing registrystudy.qs2 files older schema error load via $check_version() actionable message pointing re-running upstream generator script.

title: swereg 26.4.19
text: Skeleton R6 class (R/r6_skeleton.R). Public methods: initialize, check_version, pipeline_hash, apply_code_entry, drop_code_entry, sync_with_registry (phase 2 incremental diff), sync_randvars (phase 3 divergence-point rewind--replay), save, print. drop_code_entry uses metadata prediction via new file-level .entry_columns(reg) helper rather runtime column map. New RegistryStudy methods: $register_framework(fn) $register_randvars(name, fn) phase 1 / phase 3 registration. $code_registry_fingerprints() returning xxhash64 digests code_registry entry’s (codes, label, groups, fn_args,   combine_as) tuple. $pipeline_hash() returning single xxhash64 (framework hash, ordered randvars sequence hashes, code registry fingerprints). Answer “freshly-built skeleton look like?” $load_skeleton(batch_number) / $save_skeleton(sk) thin wrappers around Skeleton$save() supply self$data_skeleton_dir automatically, mirroring existing $load_rawbatch() / $save_rawbatch() pattern. $skeleton_pipeline_hashes() returning per-batch summary data.table skeleton’s current pipeline_hash. Useful spotting batches sync . $assert_skeletons_consistent() pre-flight check downstream consumers: errors mixed-hash partially-rebuilt state. $write_pipeline_snapshot() writing one-row TSV {data_pipeline_snapshot_dir}/{host_label}.tsv. Git-trackable, concurrency-safe (host writes file), silently skipped snapshot candidate directory configured mounted current host. $adopt_runtime_state_from() copies runtime fields (n_ids, n_batches, batch_id_list, groups_saved) another RegistryStudy without touching config fields. Used generator scripts reload disk state without silently adopting stale group_names code_registry. New RegistryStudy public fields: framework_fn, randvars_fns (named ordered list), host_label, data_pipeline_snapshot_cp ([CandidatePath]). New active binding data_pipeline_snapshot_dir resolving data_pipeline_snapshot_cp, parallel existing data_rawbatch_dir / data_skeleton_dir / data_raw_dir accessors. File-level helpers r6_registrystudy.R: .hash_function(fn) (xxhash64 list(body(fn), formals(fn)) – stable across R sessions excludes function environment), .fingerprint_entry(reg), .entry_columns(reg) (vectorized wrapper around previously-private .generated_columns_for_entry() lifted file level), .format_batch_range(batches), .process_one_batch(study, , ...) (per-batch orchestration shared process_skeletons()’s serial callr-parallel branches).

title: swereg 26.4.19
text: $load_skeleton() calls data.table::setalloccol(obj$data, n = getOption("datatable.alloccol", 4096L)) loaded Skeleton$data restore data.table -allocation slots qs2 serialization drops. Memory overhead new data.table HEADER (~8-16 bytes per slot, ~32-64 KB total) – full copy column data, stays shared reference. negligible compared per-batch skeleton size (typically multi-GB). Without refresh, subsequent := mutations inside helper functions silently reallocate strand R6 field pointing stale old-address data.table. Studies need 4096 column slots can bump via options(datatable.alloccol = 8192L) top generator script.

title: swereg 26.4.19
text: 6 existing process_skeletons test cases rewritten use new $register_framework() idiom. Added tests/testthat/test-r6_skeleton.R (74 tests), tests/testthat/test-process_skeletons_incremental.R (47 tests), tests/testthat/test-entry_columns_parity.R (10 tests), 56 new unit tests test-registrystudy.R new methods. Total test count: 800 tests passing.

title: swereg 26.4.18
text: TTEPlan schema bump (1 → 2) new constructor signature: tteplan_from_spec_and_registrystudy() now requires candidate_dir_spec, candidate_dir_tteplan, candidate_dir_results, spec_version. previous positional spec argument removed — spec YAML now read inside resolved candidate_dir_spec using filename_spec(spec_version). Existing _plan.qs2 files error load via check_version(); regenerate re-running s0_init.R project. RegistryStudy schema bump (2 → 3): private .data_{rawbatch,skeleton,raw}_dir_candidates _cache fields replaced public data_{rawbatch,skeleton,raw}_cp fields type CandidatePath. Existing registry_study_meta.qs2 files error load; regenerate re-running run_generic_create_datasets_v2.R. Stub-free -disk filenames. Project-scoped directories longer need {project_id}_ prefix files. rename : registry_study_meta.qs2 → registrystudy.qs2 {project_id}_plan.qs2 → tteplan.qs2 {project_id}_spec.xlsx → spec.xlsx {project_id}_tables.xlsx → tables.xlsx study_spec_vXXX.yaml → spec_vXXX.yaml Use dev/rename_r6_files.sh downstream MHT repo -disk migration.

title: swereg 26.4.18
text: CandidatePath R6 class (R/r6_candidate_path.R). First-class representation “directory lives one several candidate locations depending host”. Owns candidate list, resolution cache, $resolve() / $invalidate() / $is_resolved() / $print() methods. RegistryStudy TTEPlan now hold CandidatePath instances via public *_cp fields, multi-host path resolution structurally identical across classes — drift. first_existing_path(candidates, label) (exported). Generic, study-agnostic “first existing path” picker lifted old .resolve_path(). Auto-creates first candidate whose parent directory exists (unchanged old behavior). invalidate_candidate_paths(obj) (exported). Walks R6 object’s public fields calls $invalidate() every CandidatePath finds, recursing embedded R6 objects. Called RegistryStudy$save_meta() TTEPlan$save() serialization -disk files never carry saving host’s cached resolved paths. tteplan_locate_and_load(candidate_dir_tteplan) (exported). Stage scripts (s1.R, s2.R, s3.R, s4_export.R) use one-liner load tteplan.qs2 first candidate directory exists. registrystudy_load(candidate_dir_rawbatch) (exported). Paired tteplan_from_spec_and_registrystudy() s0_init.R reads registrystudy.qs2 first rawbatch directory exists current host. TTEPlan active bindings owned paths: dir_tteplan, dir_spec, dir_results_base, dir_results (appends spec_version), tteplan, spec_path, spec_xlsx, tables_xlsx, plus data_skeleton data_rawbatch delegate embedded registrystudy (duplication). Stage methods default bindings, s1/s2/s3 longer need explicit output_dir =. Host-portable skeleton_files load. tteplan_load() now refreshes plan$skeleton_files plan$registrystudy$skeleton_files every load, reapplying n_skeleton_files_limit stored plan. plan saved one host loaded another immediately points current host’s skeleton files.

title: swereg 26.4.17
text: Custom Table 1 engine: swereg now ships Table 1 builder (.swereg_table1()), replacing optional tableone dependency. TTEEnrollment$table1() returns long-format data.table class swereg_table1 supports new arguments: arm_labels — display labels two exposure arms include_smd — toggle SMD column (defaults TRUE) show_missing — annotate variable names "(missing X.X%)" instead emitting separate Missing row (defaults TRUE). Percentages non-missing levels computed non-missing denominator, sum 100 within column. Multi-level categorical SMDs follow Yang & Dalton (2012) generalisation. Forest plot Table 3: workbook’s Table 3 sheet now forest plot rendered ggplot2. Supplemental Table S{n+1} keeps full tabular IRR ETTs (per-protocol truncated). forest plot delivered high-resolution PNG (300 dpi) embedded worksheet, plus vector PDF sidecar saved next workbook. CONSORT flowcharts: .write_consort() now renders Graphviz DOT flowchart via DiagrammeR + DiagrammeRsvg + rsvg, embeds PNG worksheet, saves PNG/PDF sidecars next workbook. Falls back legacy text-table layout optional packages missing. featured_etts argument TTEPlan$export_tables(): filters Tables 2 3 user-specified subset ETT ids; supplementary tabular IRR remains unfiltered. “Exposure definitions” legend block written main table, _Exposed/_Unexposed column suffixes rewritten spec-derived arm labels featured ETTs share single enrollment. TTEPlan$reload_spec(): refreshes cosmetic spec fields (study title, enrollment names, exposure-arm labels, outcome names, ETT descriptions) YAML spec disk WITHOUT re-running upstream pipeline. Structural changes (confounders, exclusion criteria, follow-windows, matching parameters, etc.) detected reported via loud warning applied — cached results stay bound old definitions. new fields spec_reloaded_at spec_reload_skipped_diffs surfaced Provenance sheet. TTEPlan$recompute_baselines(): re-runs new Table 1 engine cached enrollment files -process, used refresh stale baseline tables upgrading swereg without re-running $s3_analyze(). $export_tables() calls lazily detects pre-26.4.17 cached Table 1 results.

title: swereg 26.4.17
text: Raw → Unimputed unweighted Unweighted → Imputed unweighted IPW → Imputed IPW IPW Truncated → Imputed IPW truncated main Table 1 sheet hides SMD column missingness annotations; supplementary panels include .

title: swereg 26.4.17
text: TTEEnrollment$table1() now returns data.table (class c("swereg_table1", "data.table", "data.frame")) instead tableone S3 object. Code introspected TableOne fields need read long-format columns instead. tableone removed Suggests. Cached results_enrollment lists produced older versions recognised refreshed lazily next $export_tables() call (output_dir required refresh). ggplot2 now Imports (Suggests) since forest plot mandatory part $export_tables(). DiagrammeR, DiagrammeRsvg, rsvg added Suggests. required CONSORT flowcharts; without $export_tables() silently falls back legacy text CONSORT.

title: swereg 26.4.16
text: setup_progress_handlers(): Pick handler_progress() format based interactive(). interactive sessions use \r-based single-line repaint (clear = TRUE, trailing newline) bar updates place like normal terminal progress bar. non-interactive sessions (RStudio background jobs, Rscript, CI) use trailing \n clear = FALSE step new line log.

title: swereg 26.4.15
text: RegistryStudy$process_skeletons(): Pass current timestamp progress message (sequential parallel paths), matching convention already used parallel_pool(). (last: :message) suffix setup_progress_handlers() format string now shows clock time last completed batch (e.g. (last: 14:35:22)) can tell glance whether job making progress frozen. Previously called p() message, (last: ) always blank.

title: swereg 26.4.14
text: setup_progress_handlers(): real reason progress never showed RStudio background jobs – progressr silently suppresses reporting non-interactive sessions unless set options("progressr.enable" = TRUE). Background jobs interactive() == FALSE, global handler installed correctly progressor() calls emitting output. Now forces option . Also restores (last: :message) format can tell bar isn’t frozen watching item label advance.

title: swereg 26.4.13
text: setup_progress_handlers(): Drop handler_rstudio / rstudioapi branch entirely. Use handler_progress() format = "[:bar] :current/:total (:percent) :elapsedfull, eta: :eta\n" clear = FALSE every context — recipe cs9::set_progressr plnr::Plan$run_all. trailing \n makes update new log line (instead \r repaint job logs can’t render), clear = FALSE keeps finished bars scrollback. Works interactive R, RStudio’s foreground console, RStudio background-job subprocesses without detection logic handler switching. handler_rstudio ineffective background-job case codebase.

title: swereg 26.4.12
text: setup_progress_handlers(): Collapse RStudio-detection logic single rstudioapi::isAvailable(child_ok = TRUE) call. child_ok = TRUE parameter handles foreground RStudio console background-job subprocesses (via IPC parent session), earlier hasFun/exists/isJob gymnastics unnecessary. Also drops now-redundant feature-test jobAdd/jobSetProgress/jobRemove — progressr::handler_rstudio requires exist anyway.

title: swereg 26.4.11
text: setup_progress_handlers(): Stop using rstudioapi::hasFun("jobAdd") feature-test job wrappers. hasFun() () short-circuits FALSE whenever isAvailable() FALSE, (b) looks name internal rstudio namespace function actually called addJob (rstudioapi::jobAdd() wrapper forwards callFun("addJob", ...)). hasFun("jobAdd") returned FALSE even systems jobAdd() works fine, caused helper fall text handler every time. Now checks rstudioapi wrapper namespace directly via exists("jobAdd", envir = asNamespace("rstudioapi"), mode = "function").

title: swereg 26.4.10
text: setup_progress_handlers(): Fix detection RStudio background-job subprocesses. Previously relied rstudioapi::isAvailable(), returns FALSE inside jobRunScript() subprocess (.Platform$GUI “RStudio” ) — scripts launched via Source Background Job fell text handler Jobs-pane progress bar appeared. Now also accepts rstudioapi::isJob() valid context; job subprocesses, rstudioapi::callFun() auto-delegates jobAdd/jobSetProgress/jobRemove back parent RStudio session via IPC, handler_rstudio works correctly.

title: swereg 26.4.9
text: setup_progress_handlers(): Helper run scripts. Feature-detects rstudioapi::jobAdd() installs progressr::handler_rstudio() available, else falls back handler_progress(). Fixes “good progress bar” problem launching run scripts via RStudio’s Source Background Job menu — default text bar renders badly job logs, RStudio handler draws proper Jobs-pane progress bar. Automatically covers every progressr-emitting method (process_skeletons, s1_*, s2_*, s3_*) per-method changes.

title: swereg 26.4.8
text: RegistryStudy$compute_population(): Compute population denominator table saved skeleton files. Counts unique persons isoyear user-specified structural variables (e.g. sex, age, register tag). Handles annual weekly skeleton rows via uniqueN(id). Produces complete grid combinations (missing cells filled zero). Saves result population.qs2 skeleton directory.

title: swereg 26.4.7
text: s3_analyze() now prints output directory path count .qs2 files processing. s3_analyze() gains ett_ids parameter run specific ETTs (e.g. ett_ids = "ETT01"). Remove heterogeneity test (het_test) s3_analyze() — single call consumed 42GB RAM 40+ minutes CPU real data, making full runs infeasible. Remove het_slot parameter tteenrollment_irr_combine() (longer needed).

title: swereg 26.4.6
text: Fix latent bug parallel_pool(): Filter(Negate(.null), results) removed NULLs shifted indices, breaking positional item_map indexing s3_analyze. Workers fail already raise errors producing output, NULL results occur.

title: swereg 26.4.6
text: Deduplicate .write_combined_rates() .write_combined_irr() shared helper .prepare_combine_data(). Extract .build_code_lookup() helper code_lookup + fmt_var construction, shared print_spec_summary() .write_spec_summary(). Call parallel::detectCores() top s3_analyze() instead twice enrollment ETT loops.

title: swereg 26.4.5
text: $s3_analyze(): New Loop 3 method TTEPlan computes analysis results (baseline characteristics, rates, IRR, heterogeneity tests) stores plan. Split enrollment-level results (table1 variants) ETT-level results (outcome-specific). Degenerate ETTs (GLM failure) caught stored skip reason instead crashing. Progress bars via progressr. $results_summary(): Print diagnostic table showing event counts IRR/rates status per ETT. $export_tables(path): Export results multi-sheet Excel workbook: enrollment overview, ETT overview, Table 1 (chosen enrollment), Tables 2-3 (combined rates/IRR), per-enrollment combined baselines (4-panel: Raw/Unweighted/IPW/IPW Truncated), CONSORT attrition sheets, supplemental rates/IRR. tteplan_load(path): Load TTEPlan disk current class definition, ensuring new methods available old serialized objects. $s1_generate_enrollments_and_ipw(resume = TRUE) $s2_generate_analysis_files_and_ipcw_pp(resume = TRUE) skip completed work based file timestamps (must <24h old).

title: swereg 26.4.5
text: Fix '' must length 1 crash enroll() skeleton file enrolled persons current enrollment. data.table evaluates j 0-row data even , giving -variables length 0 instead scalar. also produced spurious -Inf warnings max(logical(0), na.rm = TRUE) Phase B. Fix: short-circuit enroll() empty panel entry_dt 0 rows. IPCW GLM fallback: fit_and_predict() s6_ipcw_pp now uses tryCatch; falls back marginal censoring rate model fails (e.g. near-zero events).

title: swereg 26.4.3
text: parallel_pool() rewritten use processx + qs2 tempfiles instead future.callr. Worker logic moved standalone R scripts inst/ (worker_s1a.R, worker_s1b.R, worker_s2.R), launched via processx::process$new(). data passes qs2 files disk instead R’s IPC serialization, fixing loop 1b bottleneck enrolled_ids serialized N times pipe buffers. enrolled_ids now written shared tempfile. Dependencies future, future.apply, future.callr removed; processx added.

title: swereg 26.4.2
text: parallel_pool() rewritten use future.callr instead persistent callr::r_session workers. work item now runs fresh R subprocess, eliminating deadlocks caused accumulated IPC socket state. New dependencies: future, future.apply, future.callr. processx dependency removed. callr_kill_workers() removed (longer needed).

title: swereg 26.4.2
text: Rename .s2_worker() (.s3_worker()) match $s2_generate_analysis_files_and_ipcw_pp().

title: swereg 26.3.30
text: callr_pool() gains timeout_minutes parameter (default: 30). work item runs longer timeout, worker killed item retried . retry also times , callr_pool() calls stop(). Disable timeout_minutes = NULL.

title: swereg 26.3.30
text: Move mgcv Imports Suggests (used conditionally via requireNamespace()). Add @importFrom progressr utils::getFromNamespace satisfy NAMESPACE checks. Replace swereg::: calls getFromNamespace() callr worker sessions. Replace assign(..., globalenv()) package-level environment (.swereg_env). Add var <- NULL declarations data.table NSE variables. Add .vscode .Rbuildignore.

title: swereg 26.3.23
text: callr_pool() workers now self-terminate parent R session dies (e.g. OOM kill). worker spawns lightweight shell watchdog polls parent PID every 5 seconds. Previously, orphaned workers ran indefinitely manually cleaned via callr_kill_workers().

title: swereg 26.3.23
text: Critical: .s1_eligible_tuples() used first(rd_exposed) classify exposure trial period, detected MHT initiation happened first week 4-week trial period. period_width = 4, ~75% exposed people start MHT mid-period silently dropped — first trial period showed unexposed (week 1 pre-initiation), next period excluded prior MHT. Fixed using (rd_exposed, na.rm = TRUE) instead. existing no_prior_exposure exclusion correctly handles new-user restriction. Verified: eligible exposed count skeleton_001 went 19 → 84, matching old per-week pipeline. .s1_compute_attrition(): exposure classification now uses () per person-trial instead checking first eligible row. Aligns attrition reporting () fix .s1_eligible_tuples() — previously attrition flow underreported exposed counts ~4x. tteplan_validate_spec(): missing variables (confounders, outcomes, exclusion criteria, exposure) now stop() instead warning(). Previously, misspelled renamed variable silently pass validation break downstream (e.g. IPW model missing confounder). Category mismatches (values spec data) remain warnings since can occur small batches.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: .s1_compute_attrition(): fix undercounting person-trials row-level eligibility criteria (e.g. eligible_valid_exposure). old code checked first row per person-trial, missing cases exposure onset occurred first week. new approach filters eligible rows first, counts — matching logic used .s1_eligible_tuples(). .s1_compute_attrition(): fix negative exposed/comparator deltas participant flow. before_exclusions baseline now classifies exposure first row non-NA exposure per person-trial, rather first overall row (often rd_exposed = NA). Total person-trial counts remain unfiltered.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: TTE s1 pipeline: add data.table::setkey() calls eliminate redundant hash-based grouping. Skeleton reads .s1_prepare_skeleton() .s1b_worker() now set key (id, isoyearweek) (metadata-, re-sort). enroll() Phase B collapse uses keyed grouping (pid, trial_id), Phase D panel expansion uses keyed binary join instead merge().

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: callr_pool() PID files now written /tmp instead tempdir() orphaned workers crashed R sessions can discovered cleaned new sessions. callr_kill_workers() simplified orphan-cleanup: kills workers whose parent R process dead removes stale PID files. -session cleanup already handled callr_pool()’s .exit() handler; function needed hard crashes (SIGKILL, OOM).

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: callr_pool() now uses persistent callr::r_session workers instead spawning fresh callr::r_bg() process per work item. swereg namespace loaded per worker slot rather per item, eliminating redundant startup overhead scaling large numbers items. Orphan protection: callr_pool() writes PID file per invocation cleans orphaned worker sessions previous crashed runs (e.g. OOM kills) next invocation.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: Fixed 3 test failures test-tte_spec.R caused s1 pipeline changes: added missing rd_exposed column .s1_compute_attrition test fixtures, added n_exposed/n_unexposed mock attrition data, updated matching output expectations.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: s1_generate_enrollments_and_ipw() now caches prepared skeletons s1a (scout) s1b (enrollment) passes, eliminating redundant file reads exclusion processing. Expected ~30-40% reduction per-enrollment wall-clock time. .s1b_worker() now subsets skeleton enrolled persons computing derived confounders, avoiding expensive rolling-window operations non-enrolled persons. TTEEnrollment$new() accepts own_data = TRUE skip defensive data.table::copy() caller reuse data. Used .s1b_worker() skeleton discarded immediately . enroll() Phase B now aggregates confounders, time-exposure, outcome columns single groupby pass instead four separate passes merges.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: “Valid exposure” (eligible_valid_exposure) now first exclusion criterion TTE attrition flow. Rows rd_exposed NA explicitly accounted rather silently disappearing -exclusions total first real criterion. TARGET Item 8 (participant flow) now shows richer flow diagram -exclusion counts, per-step exposed/unexposed breakdown, delta (excluded) remaining counts criterion, right-justified aligned columns, color-coded output (red exclusions, cyan remaining). Post-matching line also reformatted arrow indicator. “exclusions” line longer shows meaningless exposed/comparator breakdown. enrollment_counts$attrition now includes n_exposed n_unexposed columns "before_exclusions" row.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: Fixed trial_id missing error caused attr<- breaking data.table’s internal self-reference. Replaced data.table::setattr() .s1_prepare_skeleton() tteplan_apply_exclusions() preserve -place modification semantics. Fixed callr worker stale-namespace bug: devtools::load_all() subprocess, worker functions still referenced old (installed) swereg namespace. Now rebinds worker function’s environment freshly-loaded namespace.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: Reorganized print_spec_summary() header layout: renamed “Study created” → “RegistryStudy”, merged “Skeletons created” + “Skeleton files” single nested line tree connector, renamed “Plan created” → “TTEPlan”, reordered follow data pipeline order. Rewrote TARGET checklist items 6c, 6h, 7a-h print_target_checklist() academic prose suitable copy-pasting methods section. Item 6c now dynamically reflects per-enrollment matching ratios spec.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: enrollment_counts structure changed: element TTEPlan$enrollment_counts now list $attrition $matching sub-elements (single data.table). Code accessing plan$enrollment_counts[["01"]] directly data.table must update plan$enrollment_counts[["01"]]$matching. person_trial_id renamed enrollment_person_trial_id: composite key column now 3-part name matching 3-part format (enrollment_id.person_id.trial_id). code referencing person_trial_id must updated. process_fn parameter removed $s1_generate_enrollments_and_ipw(): two-pass spec-driven pipeline now code path. self$spec required (create plans tteplan_from_spec_and_registrystudy()). legacy single-pass .s1_worker() deleted. .s2_worker() renamed .s3_worker(): Internal Loop 2 IPCW-PP worker renamed avoid confusion two-pass Loop 1 pipeline.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: Two-pass enrollment pipeline: $s1_generate_enrollments_and_ipw() now uses two-pass pipeline fixes cross-batch matching ratio imbalance: Pass 1a (scout): Lightweight parallel pass collecting eligible (person_id, trial_id, exposed) tuples batches. Centralized matching: Combines tuples performs per-trial_id matching globally, ensuring correct ratio across batches. Pass 1b (full enrollment): Parallel pass using pre-matched IDs enroll without per-batch matching. enrollment_counts TTEPlan: New field storing per-trial matching counts (total vs enrolled, exposed vs unexposed) TARGET Item 8 reporting. .assign_trial_ids(): New shared helper function single source truth isoyearweek -> trial_id mapping. Used consistently scout (s1a) enrollment (s1b/enroll) phases. enrolled_ids parameter TTEEnrollment$new(): provided, enrollment skips matching phase uses pre-decided IDs directly, enabling two-pass pipeline. Per-criterion attrition counts TARGET Item 8: scout pass (s1a) now computes cumulative person person-trial counts eligibility step. Stored plan$enrollment_counts[["01"]]$attrition long-format data.table columns trial_id, criterion, n_persons, n_person_trials. $print_target_checklist() Item 8 auto-populates counts available.

title: swereg 26.3.21
text: $heterogeneity_test(): New method TTEEnrollment tests heterogeneity treatment effects across trials via Wald test trial_id × exposure interaction (Hernán 2008, Danaei 2013). $print_target_checklist(): New method TTEPlan generates self-contained TARGET Statement (Cashin et al., JAMA 2025) 21-item reporting checklist. Auto-populates items study spec provides [FILL ] placeholders PI completion.

title: swereg 26.3.21
text: $irr() calendar-time adjustment: Outcome model now includes trial_id covariate adjust calendar-time variation outcome rates across enrollment bands (Caniglia 2023, Danaei 2013). Uses ns(trial_id, df=3) ≥5 unique trial IDs, linear term 2-4, omitted 1. $irr() IPW-guard: $irr() now rejects IPW-weight columns (ipw, ipw_trunc) per-protocol censoring applied. swereg pipeline applies per-protocol censoring $s4_prepare_for_analysis(), per-protocol weights (analysis_weight_pp_trunc) valid censored dataset.

title: swereg 26.3.21
text: Methodology vignette: New vignette("tte-methodology") maps swereg TTE implementation five reference papers (Hernán 2008/2016, Danaei 2013, Caniglia 2023, Cashin 2025). Documents methods implemented, , design rationale. Analysis types: vignette("tte-nomenclature") now documents swereg supports per-protocol analysis . ITT analysis supported pipeline censors protocol deviation. -treated analysis requires time-varying IPW (implemented). period_width documentation: vignette("tte-nomenclature") now explains enrollment band width / residual immortal time bias trade-, citing Caniglia (2023) Hernán (2016). Matching approach: vignette("tte-nomenclature") now documents per-band stratified matching design choice alternatives literature. $s2_ipw() documentation: Clarified IPW estimates propensity score baseline treatment assignment , time-varying treatment weights. $irr() documentation: Documented IRR ≈ HR rare events, ns(tstop) flexible baseline hazard, quasipoisson overdispersion, computational equivalence pooled logistic regression. IPCW stabilization: Documented simplified marginal stabilization approach relationship Danaei (2013).

title: swereg 26.3.21
text: Added tests $rates(), $irr(), $km(), $irr() trial_id, IPW-guard, IPCW formula trial_id.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: Band-based enrollment: Added explicit isoyearweek ordering band-level collapse prevent silent misclassification input data pre-sorted time. IPCW-PP: Censoring model now includes trial_id account calendar-time variation censoring patterns across enrollment bands. person_weeks: Now computed actual source row counts band collapse instead hardcoded period_width. Partial-coverage bands (e.g., data boundaries) now contribute accurate person-time.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: $irr(): Removed constant (time adjustment) Poisson model. flexible model natural splines (splines::ns(tstop, df=3)) retained. Output columns renamed: IRR_flex → IRR, IRR_flex_lower → IRR_lower, IRR_flex_upper → IRR_upper, IRR_flex_pvalue → IRR_pvalue, warn_flex → warn. IRR_const* warn_const columns removed. tteenrollment_irr_combine(): Updated match new $irr() output. Columns renamed: IRR (flexible) → IRR, 95% CI (flexible) → 95% CI, p (flexible) → p. Constant-model columns removed. TTE ID semantics: composite person-per-trial identifier column now called person_trial_id (trial_id). actual trial identifier (enrollment band) now exposed trial_id enrollment output. fixes semantics trial_id means trial person_trial_id identifies person’s participation trial. TTEDesign default: id_var default changed "trial_id" "person_trial_id". s1_impute_confounders(): longer hardcodes trial_id; uses design$id_var throughout.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: Rename private methods prepare_outcome ipcw_pp s5_prepare_outcome s6_ipcw_pp signal execution order within s4_prepare_for_analysis(). Reorder TTEEnrollment public step methods match numeric sequence (s1 s2).

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: Band-based enrollment: TTEEnrollment enrollment now uses N-week bands (controlled period_width TTEDesign, default 4). Calendar time grouped bands based isoyearweek, matching done per-band (stratified), data collapsed band level enrollment. eliminates separate $s1_collapse() step entirely. Step renumbering: Public workflow methods TTEEnrollment renumbered removing $s1_collapse(): $s2_impute_confounders() -> $s1_impute_confounders() $s3_ipw() -> $s2_ipw() $s4_truncate_weights() -> $s3_truncate_weights() $s5_prepare_for_analysis() -> $s4_prepare_for_analysis() period_width parameter: Moved TTEPlan$s1_generate_enrollments_and_ipw() TTEDesign$new(period_width = 4L). Now part design contract. isoyearweek column required: Band-based enrollment requires isoyearweek column person-week data. Schema version bump: TTEDesign TTEEnrollment schema versions bumped 2. Objects saved version 1 warn load.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: TTEPlan provenance timestamps: TTEPlan now tracks created_at (stamped construction), registry_study_created_at (source RegistryStudy), skeleton_created_at (first skeleton file’s attribute). three timestamps shown print() print_spec_summary() available, making easy detect stale plans. R6 schema versioning: R6 classes (RegistryStudy, TTEPlan, TTEDesign, TTEEnrollment) now carry .schema_version private field, stamped construction time. new $check_version() public method compares stored version current class definition warns stale. qs2_read() automatically calls $check_version() R6 objects loading, outdated serialized objects produce clear warning instead silently breaking. Deprecation warnings old add_* parameter names: add_diagnoses(diags=), add_operations(ops=), add_rx(rxs=), add_icdo3s(icdo3s=), add_snomed3s(snomed3s=), add_snomedo10s(snomedo10s=) now emit deprecation warning old parameter name used. Use codes= instead.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: RegistryStudy: register_codes() now takes declarative signature: register_codes(codes, fn, groups, fn_args, combine_as). call declares codes, function apply , data groups use, optional prefix/combine behavior. old per-type fields (icd10_codes, rx_atc_codes, rx_produkt_codes, operation_codes, icdo3_codes) old register_codes(icd10_codes = ...) signature removed. single code_registry list field replaces . summary_table(): type parameter removed. type column replaced label. Use label filter. add_diagnoses(), add_operations(), add_rx(), add_icdo3s(), add_snomed3s(), add_snomedo10s(): codes parameter renamed codes (diags, ops, rxs, icdo3s, snomed3s, snomedo10s). Old parameter names still work backwards compatibility.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: Moved qs2_read() file (R/qs2.R) inlined fallback logic directly. Removed pointless .qs_save wrapper (replaced direct qs2::qs_save calls) .qs_read internal helper.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: skeleton_save() longer splits batches sub-files. saves one file per batch skeleton_NNN.qs2 (skeleton_NNN_SS.qs2). ids_per_file id_col parameters removed. RegistryStudy: batch_sizes parameter (integer vector) replaced batch_size (single integer, default 1000). ids_per_skeleton_file parameter removed. batches now uniform size.

title: swereg 26.3.21
text: tte_rbind() → tteenrollment_rbind() tte_rates_combine() → tteenrollment_rates_combine() tte_irr_combine() → tteenrollment_irr_combine() tte_impute_confounders() → tteenrollment_impute_confounders() tte_read_spec() → tteplan_read_spec() tte_apply_exclusions() → tteplan_apply_exclusions() tte_apply_derived_confounders() → tteplan_apply_derived_confounders() tte_validate_spec() → tteplan_validate_spec() tte_plan_from_spec_and_registrystudy() → tteplan_from_spec_and_registrystudy() tte_callr_pool() → callr_pool() tte_eligible_isoyears() → skeleton_eligible_isoyears() tte_eligible_age_range() → skeleton_eligible_age_range() tte_eligible_no_events_in_window_excluding_wk0() → skeleton_eligible_no_events_in_window_excluding_wk0() tte_eligible_no_observation_in_window_excluding_wk0() → skeleton_eligible_no_observation_in_window_excluding_wk0() tte_eligible_no_events_lifetime_before_and_after_baseline() → skeleton_eligible_no_events_lifetime_before_and_after_baseline() tte_eligible_combine() → skeleton_eligible_combine()

title: swereg 26.3.21
text: RENAMED: R/tte_enrollment_r6.R → R/r6_tteenrollment.R RENAMED: R/tte_plan_r6.R → R/r6_tteplan.R RENAMED: R/registry_study_r6.R → R/r6_registry_study.R EXTRACTED: callr_pool() file R/callr_pool.R MOVED: Eligibility helpers R/skeleton_utils.R MOVED: tteenrollment_impute_confounders() R/r6_tteenrollment.R

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: RENAMED: TTEEnrollment public workflow methods now step-number prefixes signal execution order: $collapse() → $s1_collapse() $impute_confounders() → $s2_impute_confounders() $ipw() → $s3_ipw() $truncate() → $s4_truncate_weights() $prepare_for_analysis() → $s5_prepare_for_analysis() RENAMED: $s4_truncate() → $s4_truncate_weights() clarity. RENAMED: TTEPlan orchestration methods now step-number prefixes: $generate_enrollments_and_ipw() → $s1_generate_enrollments_and_ipw() $generate_analysis_files_and_ipcw_pp() → $s2_generate_analysis_files_and_ipcw_pp() RENAMED: Internal worker functions consistent naming: .tte_process_skeleton() → .s1_worker() .loop2_worker() → .s2_worker() REMOVED: Constructor wrapper functions tte_design(), tte_enrollment(), tte_plan(). Use TTEDesign$new(), TTEEnrollment$new(), TTEPlan$new() directly. auto-detection data-copy logic tte_enrollment() moved TTEEnrollment$new().

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: REFACTOR: Inlined 5 6 private helper methods single callers TTEEnrollment (.calculate_ipw, .calculate_ipcw, .combine_weights_fn, .match_ratio, .collapse_periods). Kept .truncate_weights private (used 2 places). Reduces indirection stateless methods don’t use self. TESTS: Rewrote test-tte_weights.R test public API ($s1_collapse(), $s3_ipw(), $s4_truncate(), tte_enrollment(ratio=)) instead accessing inlined private methods.

title: swereg 26.3.20
text: REFACTOR: Inlined 6 weight/matching functions private methods TTEEnrollment (tte_truncate_weights, tte_calculate_ipw, tte_calculate_ipcw, tte_combine_weights, tte_match_ratio, tte_collapse_periods). Removed 2 orphaned functions (tte_identify_censoring, tte_time_to_event). Users access functionality R6 methods ($collapse, $ipw, $truncate, etc.). REFACTOR: Consolidated TTE source files 7 2 (+1 rename): tte_design.R + tte_enrollment.R + tte_weights.R merged tte_enrollment_r6.R (TTEDesign + TTEEnrollment + weight/matching functions called methods) tte_plan.R + tte_spec.R + tte_eligibility.R merged tte_plan_r6.R (TTEPlan + spec functions + eligibility helpers) registry_study.R renamed registry_study_r6.R Files containing R6 classes now _r6 suffix discoverability REORDER: TTEEnrollment public methods now follow workflow execution order: collapse -> ipw -> impute_confounders -> truncate -> prepare_for_analysis -> extract/summary/diagnostics -> analysis output. DOCS: Added inline comments documenting data flow generate_enrollments_and_ipw() (Loop 1), .tte_process_skeleton(), private$enroll(), enrollment_spec(), add_one_ett().

title: swereg 26.3.18
text: MHT: Added rd_approach3b_{single,multiple} exposure variables collapse estrogen_progesterone_bioidentical estrogen_progesterone_synthetic single estrogen_progesterone level. Derived relabeling finished approach3 columns, valid switching active MHT types never triggers “previous”. MHT: x2026_mht_add_lmed() now creates exposure variables (rd_approach{1,2,3}_{single,multiple}) internally via new internal helper x2026_mht_create_exposure_variables(). consolidates MHT LMED logic package, eliminating need separate step 14 external workflow scripts. MHT: Removed 18 sensitivity columns (*_sensitivity_60p, *_sensitivity_under60censorallat60, *_sensitivity_under60censorrefat65) x2026_mht_create_exposure_variables(). logic issue local_or_none_mht rows age >= 65 produced NA instead FALSE. rd_age_continuous column longer required input.

title: swereg 26.2.27
text: VALIDATION: tte_validate_spec() now emits warning() instead stop() spec variables values missing skeleton. makes validation informational rather blocking, useful working small data subsets rare categories may absent.

title: swereg 26.2.22
text: EXPORTED: tte_callr_pool() — generic callr::r_bg() worker pool, generalized internal .tte_callr_pool(). New API accepts items (list arg-lists), worker_fn, item_labels, collect (FALSE discard results workers save directly). Eliminates boilerplate scripts need parallel loops (e.g., Loop 2 IPCW-PP). NEW: TTEPlan$generate_analysis_files_and_ipcw_pp() — Loop 2 method runs per-ETT IPCW-PP calculation saves analysis-ready files. Mirrors $generate_enrollments_and_ipw() (Loop 1). Parameters: output_dir, estimate_ipcw_pp_separately_by_exposure, estimate_ipcw_pp_with_gam, n_workers, swereg_dev_path.

title: swereg 26.2.22
text: MEMORY: tte_calculate_ipcw() now uses mgcv::bam(discrete = TRUE) instead mgcv::gam() use_gam = TRUE. bam() discretizes covariates avoid forming full model matrix, dramatically reducing peak memory large datasets. Model objects also explicitly freed (rm() + gc()) exposed/unexposed fits. MEMORY: $irr() $km() now subset columns needed creating survey::svydesign(). Previously full data.table (columns) copied survey object. Model objects intermediate data freed fits.

title: swereg 26.2.21
text: RENAMED: $prepare_for_analysis() parameters estimate_ipcw_separately_by_exposure → estimate_ipcw_pp_separately_by_exposure estimate_ipcw_with_gam → estimate_ipcw_pp_with_gam consistency IPCW-PP method control. PRIVATE: $enroll(), $prepare_outcome(), $ipcw_pp(), $combine_weights() now private methods TTEEnrollment. Enrollment: use tte_enrollment(data, design, ratio = 2, seed = 4) instead tte_enrollment(data, design)$enroll(ratio = 2, seed = 4). Outcome prep + IPCW: use $prepare_for_analysis() (unchanged). Weight combination: handled automatically $ipcw_pp() (unchanged). Tests can access private methods via enrollment$.__enclos_env__$private$method_name().

title: swereg 26.2.20
text: RENAMED: $prepare_analysis() → $prepare_for_analysis() TTEEnrollment. new name better communicates method prepares enrollment analysis (analysis ).

title: swereg 26.2.20
text: FIXED: 3 remaining broken test calls (tte_extract(), tte_summary(), tte_weights()) migrated R6 method syntax ($extract(), print(), $combine_weights()). Column assertion updated: "weight_pp" → "analysis_weight_pp". FIXED: $impute_confounders() now appends "impute" steps_completed, consistent mutating methods. FIXED: $ipcw_pp() IPW column guard moved IPCW computation (fail-fast).

title: swereg 26.2.20
text: FIXED: Vignette truncation bounds corrected “0.5th 99.5th percentiles” “1st 99th percentiles” (matching code defaults lower = 0.01, upper = 0.99). FIXED: TTEDesign roxygen references removed tte_match() / tte_expand() replaced $enroll(). FIXED: $weight_summary() moved “Mutating” “Non-mutating” section TTEEnrollment roxygen (prints, never modifies data).

title: swereg 26.2.13
text: NEW: $prepare_for_analysis() method TTEEnrollment merges $prepare_outcome() + $ipcw_pp() one step. Parameters: outcome, follow_up, separate_by_exposure, use_gam, censoring_var. NEW: $enrollment_stage active binding TTEEnrollment. Derives lifecycle stage existing state: "pre_enrollment" → "enrolled" → "analysis_ready". Zero maintenance — reads data_level steps_completed.

title: swereg 26.2.13
text: FIXED: 24 broken test cases calling removed standalone functions (tte_enroll(), tte_collapse(), tte_ipw(), tte_truncate(), tte_prepare_outcome()) migrated R6 method syntax. Error message patterns updated match method names (e.g., enroll() tte_enroll()).

title: swereg 26.2.12
text: RENAMED: TTETrial class → TTEEnrollment, tte_trial() → tte_enrollment(), summary.TTETrial → summary.TTEEnrollment. class represents enrollment (matching + panel expansion), individual emulated target trial (ETT). Aligns naming ETT grid concept TTEPlan.

title: swereg 26.2.11
text: REMOVED: 19 standalone TTE functions moved R6 methods TTETrial (15 methods) TTEPlan (4 methods). Pipe chaining (trial |> tte_ipw()) replaced $-chaining (trial$ipw()). TTETrial methods: $enroll(), $collapse(), $ipw(), $ipcw_pp(), $combine_weights(), $truncate(), $prepare_outcome(), $impute_confounders(), $weight_summary(), $extract(), $summary(), $table1(), $rates(), $irr(), $km(). TTEPlan methods: $add_one_ett(), $save(), $enrollment_spec(), $generate_enrollments_and_ipw(). RENAMED: TTEPlan$task() → TTEPlan$enrollment_spec(). method returns enrollment metadata (design, enrollment_id, age_range, n_threads), generic task. process_fn callback parameter convention changes function(task, file_path) function(enrollment_spec, file_path). Removed exports: tte_enroll, tte_collapse, tte_ipw, tte_ipcw_pp, tte_weights, tte_truncate, tte_prepare_outcome, tte_extract, tte_summary, tte_weight_summary, tte_table1, tte_rates, tte_irr, tte_km, tte_plan_add_one_ett, tte_plan_save, tte_plan_task, tte_generate_enrollments_and_ipw. Kept standalone: tte_rbind(), tte_rates_combine(), tte_irr_combine(), tte_impute_confounders() (thin wrapper callback default). CHANGED: TTE classes (TTEDesign, TTETrial, TTEPlan) migrated S7 R6. Property access changes @ $ (e.g., trial@data → trial$data, design@id_var → design$id_var). R6 reference semantics eliminate copy--write overhead trial$data[, := ...], reducing peak RAM ~3X ~2X weight-calculation chain (Loop 2). FIXED: Three S7 @ accessor bugs silently produced -ops: $ipcw_pp(): dropping intermediate IPCW columns (p_censor, etc.) $collapse(): creating person_weeks column $impute_confounders(): deleting old confounder columns merge fixed automatically R6 (-place modification works). CHANGED: $ipcw_pp() now inlines weight combination truncation (calling tte_combine_weights() tte_truncate_weights() via function parameters created extra refcount). Keeps data.table refcount=1 throughout.

title: swereg 26.2.11
text: Split tte_classes.R tte_methods.R per-class files methods inline: tte_design.R, tte_trial.R, tte_plan.R. tte_generate.R reduced thin tte_impute_confounders() wrapper + .tte_callr_pool() helper. Added S3method(summary, TTETrial) → delegates $summary().

title: swereg 26.2.11
text: ADDED: R6 package Imports (S7 retained skeleton classes).

title: swereg 26.2.10
text: FIXED: tte_ipw(), tte_ipcw_pp(): -place joins via S7 @ accessor now use extract/modify/reassign pattern (dt <- trial@data; dt[...]; trial@data <- dt). previous trial@data[, := ...] silently modified copy, leaving S7 object’s data unchanged.

title: swereg 26.2.10
text: IMPROVED: tte_ipw(), tte_ipcw_pp(), tte_calculate_ipcw(): replace merge() -place keyed joins (data[, := ...]), reducing peak RAM ~3x ~2x panel size weight-calculation chain.

title: swereg 26.2.10
text: CHANGED: tte_ipcw_pp() now also combines weights (ipw * ipcw_pp → analysis_weight_pp), truncates analysis_weight_pp, drops intermediate IPCW columns (p_censor, p_uncensored, cum_p_uncensored, marginal_p, cum_marginal). Callers longer need tte_weights() + tte_truncate() tte_ipcw_pp(). RENAMED: tte_generate_enrollments() → tte_generate_enrollments_and_ipw(). Now computes IPW + truncation full combined enrollment (imputation), per-ETT Loop 2 longer needs call tte_ipw(). New stabilize parameter (default TRUE) controls IPW stabilization.

title: swereg 26.2.10
text: NEW: tte_plan_load() reads .qs2 plan file reconstructs TTEPlan S7 object. Companion tte_plan_save(). CHANGED: tte_plan_save() now persists project_prefix skeleton_files alongside ett global_max_isoyearweek, tte_plan_load() can fully reconstruct object. NEW: skeleton_process() gains n_workers parameter parallel batch processing. > 1, uses callr::r() + parallel::mclapply() process batches concurrently avoiding fork() + data.table OpenMP segfaults.

title: swereg 26.2.9
text: CHANGED: Migrate serialization qs (archived) qs2. .qs_save/.qs_read wrappers now call qs2::qs_save/qs2::qs_read (standard format, preserves S7 objects). file extensions changed .qs .qs2. preset parameter longer used. IMPROVED: tte_rates() now sets swereg_type exposure_var attributes output; tte_irr() sets swereg_type. RENAMED: tte_rates_table() → tte_rates_combine(), tte_irr_table() → tte_irr_combine(). New API accepts (results, slot, descriptions) — extracts rates/irr slot internally, removing need lapply(results, [[, "table2") call sites. Exposure column now read exposure_var attribute instead guessing via setdiff().

title: swereg 26.2.9
text: CHANGED: tte_plan_add_one_ett() now requires explicit enrollment_id parameter. Auto-assignment based follow_up + age_group removed. Validation design params match within enrollment_id preserved. IMPROVED: print(plan) now shows enrollment grid full ETT grid. CHANGED: tte_plan_add_one_ett() bundles age_group, age_min, age_max, person_id_var argset named list parameter. time_exposure_var eligible_var longer defaults (must explicit). exposure_var removed interface (hardcoded "baseline_exposed"). RENAMED: file_id column ett data.table → enrollment_id. makes explicit ETTs sharing follow_up + age_group processed together one “enrollment” (shared eligibility, matching, collapse, imputation). RENAMED: tte_generate_trials() → tte_generate_enrollments(). function generates enrollments (one per follow_up × age_group), individual trials. RENAMED: tte_plan_task() return list key file_id → enrollment_id. UPDATED: print(plan) now shows “Enrollments: N x M skeleton files” instead “Tasks: N file_id(s) x M skeleton files”.

title: swereg 26.2.8
text: CHANGED: tte_plan() now infrastructure-— takes project_prefix, skeleton_files, global_max_isoyearweek. Use tte_plan_add_one_ett() add ETTs per-ETT design parameters. REMOVED: TTEPlan plan-level properties confounder_vars, person_id_var, exposure_var, time_exposure_var, eligible_var. now per-ETT columns ett data.table. REMOVED: Internal .tte_grid() function. ETT grid now built incrementally via tte_plan_add_one_ett(). ADDED: TTEPlan@project_prefix property (needed file naming tte_plan_add_one_ett()).

title: swereg 26.2.8
text: NEW: tte_plan_add_one_ett() — builder function adds one ETT row plan. Stores design params (confounder_vars, person_id_var, exposure_var, time_exposure_var, eligible_var) per-ETT, allowing different ETTs use different confounders. Validates design params match within enrollment_id (follow_up + age_group). RENAMED: TTEPlan@files property → TTEPlan@skeleton_files clarity.

title: swereg 26.2.7
text: REFACTORED: tte_generate_enrollments() (formerly tte_generate_trials()) now takes TTEPlan object instead separate parameters (ett, files, confounder_vars, global_max_isoyearweek). process_fn callback signature changes function(file_path, design, file_id, age_range, n_threads) function(task, file_path) task list design, enrollment_id, age_range, n_threads.

title: swereg 26.2.7
text: tte_plan(): Constructor function tte_plan_task(plan, ): Extract -th enrollment task list design, enrollment_id, age_range, n_threads plan[[]]: Shorthand tte_plan_task(plan, ) length(plan): Number unique enrollment_id groups Supports interactive testing: task <- plan[[1]]; process_fn(task, plan@skeleton_files[1])

title: swereg 26.2.6
text: FIXED: Add missing topics pkgdown reference index (TTEDesign, TTETrial, x2026_mht_add_lmed)

title: swereg 26.2.5
text: FIXED: Set eval = FALSE skeleton3-analyze vignette prevent build errors optional qs package dependency

title: swereg 26.2.4
text: FIXED: Remove qs Suggests fix GitHub Actions CI (package available CRAN)

title: swereg 26.2.3
text: Old workflow: tte_trial(data, design) |> tte_match(ratio = 2, seed = 4) |> tte_expand(extra_cols = "isoyearweek") New workflow: tte_trial(data, design) |> tte_enroll(ratio = 2, seed = 4, extra_cols = "isoyearweek") two operations tightly coupled always used together tte_enroll() combines sampling (matching) panel expansion one step Records “enroll” steps_completed (previously recorded “match” “expand”)

title: swereg 26.2.3
text: tte_eligible_isoyears(): Check eligibility based calendar years tte_eligible_age_range(): Check eligibility based age range tte_eligible_no_events_in_window_excluding_wk0(): Check events prior window (correctly excludes baseline week) tte_eligible_no_observation_in_window_excluding_wk0(): Check specific value prior window (categorical variables) tte_eligible_combine(): Combine multiple eligibility columns using logic functions modify data.tables reference return invisibly method chaining

title: swereg 26.2.3
text: IMPROVED: Clarified eligibility checks EXCLUDE baseline week. Using cumsum(x) == 0 incorrect includes current week. new eligibility functions use any_events_prior_to() correctly excludes current row.

title: swereg 26.1.31
text: TTEDesign class: Define column name mappings reuse across TTE functions TTETrial class: Fluent method chaining workflow state tracking tte_design() / tte_trial(): Constructor functions S7 classes tte_match(), tte_expand(), tte_collapse(), tte_ipw(): S7 methods data preparation tte_prepare_outcome(), tte_ipcw(): Outcome-specific per-protocol analysis tte_weights(), tte_truncate(): Weight combination truncation tte_rbind(): Combine batched trial objects tte_extract(), tte_summary(): Access data diagnostics tte_table1(), tte_rates(), tte_irr(), tte_km(): Analysis visualization

title: swereg 26.1.31
text: tte_tte(): Use tte_prepare_outcome() computes weeks_to_event internally tte_set_outcome(): Use tte_prepare_outcome(outcome = "...") instead tte_censoring(): Use tte_prepare_outcome() handles censoring internally

title: swereg 26.1.31
text: ADDED: S7 package Imports object-oriented class system

title: swereg 26.1.30
text: tte_calculate_ipw(): Calculate stabilized inverse probability treatment weights (IPW) baseline confounding adjustment using propensity scores tte_calculate_ipcw(): Calculate time-varying inverse probability censoring weights (IPCW) per-protocol analysis using GAM GLM tte_identify_censoring(): Identify protocol deviation loss follow-per-protocol analysis tte_combine_weights(): Combine IPW IPCW weights per-protocol effect estimation tte_truncate_weights(): Truncate extreme weights specified quantiles reduce variance tte_match_ratio(): Sample comparison group specified ratio (e.g., 2:1 unexposed exposed) tte_collapse_periods(): Collapse fine-grained time intervals (e.g., weekly) coarser periods (e.g., 4-week) tte_time_to_event(): Calculate time first event trial/person

what: New Swedish Date Parsing and Enhanced Data Cleaning
title: swereg 25.7.16
text: Handles 4-character (YYYY), 6-character (YYYYMM), 8-character (YYYYMMDD) formats Automatically replaces “0000” “0701” “00” “15” missing date components Supports custom defaults missing date parts Includes comprehensive error handling vectorized processing New date_column parameter specify column contains dates Automatically creates cleaned ‘date’ column using parse_swedish_date() Works default data.table methods Maintains backward compatibility existing code add_diagnoses(), add_operations(), add_rx(), add_cods() expect ‘date’ column Clear error messages guide users use make_lowercase_names(data, date_column = "...") Improved validation ensures data preprocessing consistency Annual rows (is_isoyear==TRUE) personyears = 1 Weekly rows (is_isoyear==FALSE) personyears = 1/52.25 Facilitates person-time calculations survival analysis Uses weekly data instead yearly data precise analyses Age calculation based isoyearweeksun instead isoyear Includes person-time descriptive statistics Demonstrates proper use new date cleaning workflow
